messages.text: If I am correct, then the credit is due to Allah. And if I am mistaken, then it is from myself and from Shaytan, for I am not infallible. And if I ever spread something that calls to other than the haqq or ṣawab, then I ask for sincere advice, and I pray for whoever advises and corrects me. May Allah reward you with goodness.

text.text: Sheikh Muqbil al-Wadi‘ī – may Allah have mercy on him said:

text: “What I advise myself and everyone else is to occupy oneself with knowledge, for the concerns of this world have no end. Beware of being preoccupied with life’s problems; if we were, we would not be able to seek knowledge.”

text.text: Al-Bashā’ir fī al-Samā‘ al-Mubāshir (p.21)
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: ʿIlm al-Kalām
By Ash-Shaykha Umm ʿAbdillah Al-Wādiʿiyyah حفظها الله
She said:

text:
I asked my father رحمه الله: “What is ʿIlm al-Kalām?”
He replied: ʿIlm al-Kalām is a set of methods and rules by which they claim to arrive at knowing the ‘aqīdah.

Then he رحمه الله clarified that ʿIlm al-Kalām is blameworthy, and he said:

text.text: Ash-Shāfiʿī رحمه الله said:

“My view and my stance regarding the people of Kalām is that they should be beaten with palm branches, then made to ride on camels, and paraded among the tribes and clans, and it should be announced over them: This is the punishment of the one who abandoned the Book and the Sunnah and turned to ʿIlm al-Kalām.”
(Reported by Abū Nuʿaym Ahmad ibn ʿAbdullah in Ḥilyat al-Awliyāʾ 9/104)

text: The great scholars of Kalām themselves admitted that ʿIlm al-Kalām leads to confusion and results in doubts.

text.text: Muhammad ibn 'Umar ar-Rāzī said:

text:
نِهَايَةُ إِقْدَامِ الْعُقُولِ عِقَالُ *** وَأَكْثَرُ سَعْيِ الْعَالَمِينَ ضَلَالُ
وَأَرْوَاحُنَا فِي وَحْشَةٍ مِّنْ جُسُومِنَا *** وَحَاصِلُ دُنْيَانَا أَذًى وَوَبَالُ
وَلَمْ نَسْتَفِدْ مِنْ بَحْثِنَا طُولَ عُمْرِنَا *** سِوَى أَنْ جَمَعْنَا فِيهِ قِيلَ وَقَالُوا

"The end result of advancing the intellect is restriction. And the majority of the efforts of mankind lead to misguidance. Our souls remain estranged from our bodies. And all we gained from this world is harm and burden. We did not gain from our lifelong research except compiling “so-and-so said” and “they said.”"

text.text: Then he said:

text:
“I have reflected upon the paths of the theologians and the methods of the philosophers, and I did not find them to cure any sick person, nor to quench any thirsty one.”

It is said that

text.text: Abū Ḥāmid al-Ghazālī

text: and

text.text: Muhammad ibn 'Umar ar-Rāzī

text: repented, but even if that was the case, their poisonous books remained.

text.text: Ash-Shahrastānī said:

text:
لَعَمْرِي لَقَدْ طُفْتُ الْمَعَاهِدَ كُلَّهَا *** وَسَيَّرْتُ طَرْفِي بَيْنَ تِلْكَ الْمَعَالِمِ
فَلَمْ أَرَ إِلَّا وَاضِعًا كَفَّ حَائِرٍ *** عَلَى ذَقَنٍ أَوْ قَارِعًا سِنَّ نَادِمٍ

"By my life, I have wandered through all the places of knowledge, and surveyed all of their landmarks, and I saw none except one placing his hand on his chin in bewilderment, or striking his teeth in regret."

text.text: Imām aṣ-Ṣanʿānī responded:

text:
لَعَلَّكَ أَهْمَلْتَ الطَّوَافَ بِمَعْهَدِ *** الرَّسُولِ وَمَنْ لَاقَاهُ مِنْ كُلِّ عَالِمِ
فَمَا حَارَ مَنْ يُهْدَى بِهَدْيِ مُحَمَّدٍ *** وَلَسْتَ تَرَاهُ قَارِعًا سَنَّ نَادِمِ

"Perhaps you neglected to make rounds in the institution of the Messenger, and those scholars who met him and received from him. For no one is ever confused who is guided by the guidance of Muhammad, and you will never see him striking his teeth in regret."

End of Shaykh Muqbil's words.

text.text: She said:
I say:

text: The scholars have agreed upon criticizing the people of Kalām.

text.text: Ibn ʿAbd al-Barr رحمه الله said in Jāmiʿ Bayān al-ʿIlm wa-Faḍlihi (2/942):

text: “The scholars of fiqh and the people of narrations in all regions have agreed that the people of Kalām are people of innovation and deviation. They are not counted among the ranks of the scholars. Rather, the scholars are the people of athar and understanding of it, and they excel over each other in precision, skill, and comprehension.”

text.text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

text.text: Shaykh Ibn ʿUthaymīn رحمه الله said:

text: “Try to stay away from everything that brings worry, sadness, and distress, so that you will always be at ease, with an open and relaxed heart, turning toward Allah and His worship, and toward your worldly and religious affairs.”

text.text: Sharḥ Bulūgh al-Marām (5/533)
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

text_entities.text: Shaykh Ibn ʿUthaymīn رحمه الله said:

Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: Shaykh Ibn al-‘Uthaymīn (may Allah have mercy on him) said:

text: “And know that if you choose a woman of religion, seeking guidance by the statement of the Prophet ﷺ, then Allah will make the one who was not beautiful, beautiful.”

text.text: Sharḥ Ṣaḥīḥ Muslim (7/239)
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

text_entities.text: Shaykh Ibn al-‘Uthaymīn (may Allah have mercy on him) said:

Sharḥ Ṣaḥīḥ Muslim (7/239)
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: Shaykh al-Albani رحمه اللّٰه said:

text: "The truth is heavy. So, do not make it heavier with your bad manners. I used to believe that the (main) problem in the Ummah is 'aqidah, but it became clear to me that it is 'aqidah and manners."

text.text: Source: Silsila al-Huda wa al-Nur, Tape 900
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: On Madhhab-following (Taqlīd of a school of thought):

text:
Know, O woman who calls to Allah and teaches (may Allah grant you success), that adhering to a madhhab (school of thought) has no basis in the Sharīʿah. The matter is about following the Qur’an and the Sunnah. These madhāhib came later. Imām al-Bukhārī did not belong to any of the madhāhib, nor did Imām Muslim, nor others from the earlier generations. There was no affiliation to a person. They did not ascribe themselves to Abū Bakr al-Ṣiddīq, nor to ʿUmar, nor to ʿUthmān, nor to ʿAlī. Thus, madhhab-following is something Allah has not sent down any authority for.

And praise be to Allah who has made us see the truth and made madhhabism and partisanship hateful to us.

Indeed, among the blind followers of madhhabs are those who even reject clear evidence. It is said to them: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said such and such. They reply: But my madhhab is Mālikī, or Shāfiʿī… Did Allah command us to follow the Mālikī madhhab, or the Shāfiʿī, or the Ḥanafī, or the Ḥanbalī? And they reject evidence simply because their madhhab is different.

But Allah the Exalted says:

text.text: “Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, and do not follow other guardians besides Him. Little do you remember.”

text:
(Sūrat al-Aʿrāf 7:3)

And He says:

text.text: "So let those beware who oppose his command, lest a trial befall them or a painful punishment befall them.”

text:
(Sūrat al-Nūr 24:63)

And praise be to Allah who has saved us from the trial of partisanships.

And my father (may Allah have mercy on him) said in response to a question (Fatāwā al-Ḥaramayn, no. 490):

text.text: “These madhhabs are a bidʿah with which Allah tested Islām. And I know that many of the doctors, and many of the wearers of cloaks, and many of those with turbans do not like my statement. But if I please my Lord, I do not care about their anger.”

text: Therefore, it is upon the woman who calls to Allah and teaches to be keen on attaching the students and those she calls to the evidences, and never to shift away from them to the statement of so-and-so or the madhhab of so-and-so—while maintaining respect and reverence for the scholars.

text.text: – Shaykha Umm ʿAbdillāh al-Wādiʿiyyah (may Allah preserve her)
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

The matter is about following the Qur’an and the Sunnah. These madhāhib came later. Imām al-Bukhārī did not belong to any of the madhāhib, nor did Imām Muslim, nor others from the earlier generations. There was no affiliation to a person. They did not ascribe themselves to Abū Bakr al-Ṣiddīq, nor to ʿUmar, nor to ʿUthmān, nor to ʿAlī. Thus, madhhab-following is something Allah has not sent down any authority for.

And praise be to Allah who has made us see the truth and made madhhabism and partisanship hateful to us.

Indeed, among the blind followers of madhhabs are those who even reject clear evidence. It is said to them: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said such and such. But Allah the Exalted says:
“Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, and do not follow other guardians besides Him. Little do you remember.”

(Sūrat al-Aʿrāf 7:3)

And He says:
"So let those beware who oppose his command, lest a trial befall them or a painful punishment befall them.”

(Sūrat al-Nūr 24:63)

And praise be to Allah who has saved us from the trial of partisanships.

And my father (may Allah have mercy on him) said in response to a question (Fatāwā al-Ḥaramayn, no. 490):

“These madhhabs are a bidʿah with which Allah tested Islām. And I know that many of the doctors, and many of the wearers of cloaks, and many of those with turbans do not like my statement. But if I please my Lord, I do not care about their anger.”

– Shaykha Umm ʿAbdillāh al-Wādiʿiyyah (may Allah preserve her)
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
Today, people boast about their knowledge and pass it around without any concern for acting upon it. They strive for a large following and fame, forgetting that what they have learned will be a burden and a source of harm against them on the Day of Judgment...

Al-Marroudhi, may Allah have mercy on him, said: “Ahmad told me: ‘I never wrote down a hadith from the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, without acting upon it. For example, I came across a hadith that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, had cupping (Hijama) done and gave Abu Tayyiba, the cupper, a dinar. So I had cupping done and gave the cupper a dinar.’”

- | Al-Jami’ by Al-Khatib (1/1480) | -

- Amr ibn Qays, may Allah have mercy on him, said: “If you hear something good, act upon it, and you will be among its people, even if only once.”

- | Az-Zuhd by Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal (223) | -

text.text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
The Messenger of Allah ﷺ slept on a mat. When he got up, it had left an impression on his side. We said: “O Messenger of Allah, shall we make a mattress for you?”
He said: “What is there for me with the world? I am in this world only like a traveler who takes shelter under a tree for a while, then moves on and leaves it.”

text.text: — Sahih al-Tirmidhi
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: Al-Ḥāfiẓ al-Khaṭīb al-Baghdādī رحمه الله said:

text:
“It befits the one whose time has been expanded for him, whose body Allāh the Exalted has kept sound,
and whom He has made to love rising above the ranks of the ignorant,
and placed in his heart the determination to gain understanding in the religion—
that he should seize the opportunity to hasten toward that,
out of fear that something may occur which will cut him off from it.”

text.text: [al-Faqīh wal-Mutafaqqih 2/170]
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
Shaykh Ibn Bāz رحمه الله said:

“If a person does not attend lessons of knowledge, nor listen to sermons, nor pay attention to what is conveyed from the people of knowledge, then his heedlessness increases. And his heart may even harden until it becomes sealed and stamped, and he becomes among the heedless.”

📚 Majmū‘ al-Fatāwā (12/324)

text.text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
Ibn Uthaymeen رحمه الله said:

"If a woman is forbidden from stamping her feet, for fear that a man might be tempted by the sound of her anklets and the like, then how much more so is it forbidden to uncover her face?" 

[The Message of the Hijab (9)]

text.text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
Ibn Al Qayyim رحمه الله said:

"The lover of the dunya does not get rid of three:

Distress.
Constant weariness.
Regret that doesn't end.”

إغاثة اللهفان ١/٥٨

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text: بـسمِ اللّٰـه الرَّحمـٰن الرَّحـيم

text.text: The majority of the scholars hold that depicting images of beings with souls is prohibited absolutely.

text:
Among them is Imām Aḥmad, based on the generality of the evidences.

Shaykh Ibn Bāz has a treatise titled “The Prohibition of Depicting Beings with Souls.”

Shaykh Ḥammūd al-Tuwayjirī has a treatise titled “Proclaiming Denunciation Against Those Infatuated with Photography” — he has passed away, may Allah have mercy on him. It is a very valuable treatise.

Shaykh Ibn Bāz wrote the introduction to this treatise and mentioned therein that the one who differentiated between photographic images and sculpted images — in other words, between images that have a shadow and those that do not — committed a grave error.

Because the authentic aḥādīth encompass both types, as Shaykh Ibn Bāz رحمه الله states.

This treatise was introduced by Shaykh Ibn Bāz, and he urged that it be read from beginning to end and that its meaning be understood.

Then the criterion is the evidence,

text.text: and everyone’s statement is accepted or rejected except that of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.

text:
On this issue there is a large body of evidence proving its prohibition.

Al-Bukhārī (5963) and Muslim (2110) narrated from Ibn ʿAbbās رضي الله عنهما that he said:

text.text:
I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say:
“Whoever makes an image in this world will be commanded to breathe life into it on the Day of Resurrection, and he will not be able to do so.”

text:
The word “image” is indefinite in the context of a conditional clause, which indicates generality — whether one depicts an animal or a human, whether it has a shadow or does not.

The one who makes images is imitating the Lord of the Worlds.

Al-Bukhārī (5953) and Muslim (2111) narrated from Abū Hurayrah رضي الله عنه that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say:

text.text: “And who is more unjust than one who goes to create like My creation? Let them create a grain, or let them create an atom.”

text: Muslim (969) narrated in his Ṣaḥīḥ from Abū al-Hayyāj al-Asadī who said: ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib رضي الله عنه said to me:

text.text: “Shall I not send you on the same mission that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent me on? That you do not leave any statue without obliterating it, and no raised grave without leveling it.”

text: In another narration of Muslim:

text.text: “Nor any image except that you erase it.”

text: Al-Tirmidhī (2574) narrated from Abū Hurayrah رضي الله عنه that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:

text.text: “A neck will emerge from the Fire on the Day of Resurrection, having two eyes with which it sees, two ears with which it hears, and a tongue with which it speaks. It will say: ‘I have been entrusted with three: every tyrannical obstinate one, everyone who calls upon another deity besides Allah, and the image-makers.’”

text:
The ḥadīth is authentic.

The evidences are general, whether the image is of an animal or a human, whether it has a shadow or does not, whether it is three-dimensional or not.

As for that which has a shadow, al-Nawawī said in Sharḥ Ṣaḥīḥ Muslim:

text.text: “They unanimously agreed upon the prohibition of that which has a shadow and the obligation of changing it.”

text:
As for that which does not have a shadow, such as images in cars, on walls, or on clothing, then it is prohibited according to what the evidences necessitate.

Al-Nawawī attributed the prohibition in Sharḥ Ṣaḥīḥ Muslim to the majority of the scholars, and this is the view of Aḥmad.

Al-Nawawī رحمه الله said in Sharḥ Ṣaḥīḥ Muslim:

text.text: “There is no difference in all of this — meaning in the prohibition — between what has a shadow and what does not have a shadow. This is a summary of our madhhab on the issue, and with its meaning spoke the vast majority of scholars among the Companions, the Tābiʿīn, and those after them. It is the madhhab of al-Thawrī, Mālik, Abū Ḥanīfah, and others.”

text: When it becomes clear that the majority are upon the prohibition of depicting beings with souls, whether they have a shadow or not:

text_entities.text: بـسمِ اللّٰـه الرَّحمـٰن الرَّحـيم

Because the authentic aḥādīth encompass both types, as Shaykh Ibn Bāz رحمه الله states.

This treatise was introduced by Shaykh Ibn Bāz, and he urged that it be read from beginning to end and that its meaning be understood.

Then the criterion is the evidence,
and everyone’s statement is accepted or rejected except that of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.

On this issue there is a large body of evidence proving its prohibition.

Al-Bukhārī (5963) and Muslim (2110) narrated from Ibn ʿAbbās رضي الله عنهما that he said:

I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say:
“Whoever makes an image in this world will be commanded to breathe life into it on the Day of Resurrection, and he will not be able to do so.”

“And who is more unjust than one who goes to create like My creation? Let them create a grain, or let them create an atom.”
Muslim (969) narrated in his Ṣaḥīḥ from Abū al-Hayyāj al-Asadī who said: ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib رضي الله عنه said to me:

In another narration of Muslim:
“Nor any image except that you erase it.”
Al-Tirmidhī (2574) narrated from Abū Hurayrah رضي الله عنه that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:

“A neck will emerge from the Fire on the Day of Resurrection, having two eyes with which it sees, two ears with which it hears, and a tongue with which it speaks. It will say: ‘I have been entrusted with three: every tyrannical obstinate one, everyone who calls upon another deity besides Allah, and the image-makers.’”

The ḥadīth is authentic.

The evidences are general, whether the image is of an animal or a human, whether it has a shadow or does not, whether it is three-dimensional or not.

“There is no difference in all of this — meaning in the prohibition — between what has a shadow and what does not have a shadow. This is a summary of our madhhab on the issue, and with its meaning spoke the vast majority of scholars among the Companions, the Tābiʿīn, and those after them. It is the madhhab of al-Thawrī, Mālik, Abū Ḥanīfah, and others.”
When it becomes clear that the majority are upon the prohibition of depicting beings with souls, whether they have a shadow or not:

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
As for those who say that what does not have a shadow is permissible, they are refuted by the general evidences and by specific evidences such as the ḥadīth of the cushion (namraqah).

Al-Bukhārī (5181) and Muslim (2107) narrated from ʿĀʾishah رضي الله عنها, the wife of the Prophet ﷺ, that she said: She informed him that she bought a cushion containing images. When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw it, he stood at the door and did not enter. I recognized displeasure on his face, so I said: “O Messenger of Allah, I repent to Allah and to His Messenger. What sin have I committed?” He said: “What is this cushion?” She said: “I bought it for you so that you may sit on it and recline upon it.” The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:

text.text: “Indeed, the makers of these images will be punished on the Day of Resurrection, and it will be said to them: ‘Give life to what you have created.’”

text: And he said:

text.text: “Indeed, the house in which there are images, the angels do not enter it.”

text: Al-Nawawī said in Sharḥ Ṣaḥīḥ Muslim:

text.text: “Some of the Salaf said: Prohibition applies only to what has a shadow, and there is no harm in images that have no shadow. This is a false madhhab, for the curtain in which the Prophet ﷺ denounced the image is unquestionably blameworthy, and its image had no shadow — along with the rest of the unrestricted aḥādīth concerning every image.”

text:
I say:

Imām Aḥmad also held the view of prohibition. Ibn Qudāmah said in al-Mughnī (7/285):

It was said to Abū ʿAbdillāh: “A man rents a house in which there are images — do you think he should scrape them off?” He said: “Yes.”

Al-Marwadhī said: I said to Abū ʿAbdillāh: “I entered a bathhouse and saw an image — do you think I should remove the head?” He said: “Yes.”

This indicates that it was an image without a shadow.

And Allah knows best.

text.text: — By Shaykha Umm ʿAbdillāh al-Wādiʿiyyah, may Allah preserve her
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text:
السلام عليكم،
What is the ruling on using the heart symbol (❤)?
In the Name of Allah, the Most Merciful, the Most Compassionate.

text:
There is a fatwa by the Permanent Committee (24/92), headed by Shaykh Ibn Bāz رحمه الله and Shaykh Ṣāliḥ al-Fawzān حفظه الله, in response to a question that was presented to them along with other issues. The fatwa stated:

That it involves imitation of the people of immorality, who take such symbols as an indication of their love and forbidden passion for others, becoming absorbed in it without regard for the ruling of the pure Sharīʿah concerning it. End quote (paraphrased).

After the ruling has been established that it is imitation of the people of immorality, the ḥadīth of the Prophet ﷺ is well known:

text.text: “Whoever imitates a people is one of them.”

text: My advice to myself and to everyone who reads this message is to refer, in our doubts and issues, back to the firmly grounded scholars, so that we may be upon light and insight in the matters of our religion. For Allah the Mighty and Majestic says:

text.text:
“And when there comes to them a matter of security or fear, they spread it. But if they had referred it to the Messenger and to those in authority among them, then those who are able to draw correct conclusions from it would have known it.”
(Qur’an 4:83)

text:
Umm ʿAbdillāh, daughter of Shaykh Muqbil رحمه الله
The tenth night of the month of Muḥarram, 1437 AH.

Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.text:
Abu Darda رضي الله عنه said:

"Indeed, my greatest fear concerning standing on the Day of Judgement is that it will be said to me: You have learned, hence what have you done with your knowledge?"

(Jami' Al-Bayan al-'Ilm wa Fadlih, No. 647)

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
messages.text:
On the authority of Abū Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه),
he said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said:
“Our Lord, Blessed and Exalted, descends every night to the lowest heaven when the last third of the night remains, and He says:
‘Who is calling upon Me, so that I may answer him?
Who is asking Me, so that I may give him?
Who is seeking My forgiveness, so that I may forgive him?’”
— Ṣaḥīḥ al-Bukhārī (1145)

text_entities.text:

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text: Al-Ḥasan al-Baṣrī رحمه الله said, describing the dunya:

text.text:
“It is but dreams of sleep, or like a fleeting shadow;
the intelligent person is not deceived by such a thing.”

text: Encyclopedia of Ibn Abī al-Dunyā (29/5)

text.text: Ibn al-Qayyim رحمه الله said:

text: “Acting without sincerity and without following (the Sunnah) is like a traveler who fills his bag with sand – it only weighs him down and does not benefit him.”

text.text: Al-Fawā’id (p. 66)

text_entities.text: Ibn al-Qayyim رحمه الله said:

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: Seeking Refuge with Allah from Tribulations (Fitnah)

text: On the authority of Zayd ibn Thābit رضي الله عنه from the Prophet ﷺ — in the end of the ḥadīth he said:

text.text:
“Seek refuge with Allah from tribulations, those that are apparent and those that are hidden.”
They said: We seek refuge with Allah from tribulations, those that are apparent and those that are hidden.
He said: “Seek refuge with Allah from the tribulation of the Dajjāl.”
They said: We seek refuge with Allah from the tribulation of the Dajjāl.
رواه مسلم (2867)

text: this ḥadīth is the command to seek refuge from tribulations, both apparent and hidden.

text.text: “What is apparent and what is hidden”

text:
means: what is done openly and what is concealed. It is also said: what occurs outwardly upon a person, and what exists in the heart such as shirk (polytheism), showing off (riyā’), envy, and other blameworthy inner thoughts.
(Mirqāt al-Mafātīḥ 1/209)

This supplication is among the greatest of duʿā’ for seeking refuge from tribulations and their causes.
It includes asking for steadfastness, and it is general—covering all forms of tribulations.
Tribulations are many, and the later the time, the more they increase—becoming numerous, like pieces of a dark night, surging like the waves of the sea.
Among these tribulations are:

text.text: • The tribulation of killing and fighting

text: Al-Bukhārī (447) narrates from Abū Saʿīd al-Khudrī رضي الله عنه:

text.text:
We used to carry bricks one by one, while ʿAmmār carried two at a time. The Prophet ﷺ saw him, brushed the dust off him and said:
“Woe to ʿAmmār! He will be killed by the transgressing group. He calls them to Paradise, and they call him to the Fire.” 
Abū Saʿīd said: ʿAmmār used to say: “I seek refuge with Allah from tribulations.”

• The tribulation of the world and its desires
• The tribulation of illnesses and accidents
• The tribulation of losing loved ones and things dear to the soul
• The tribulation of children
• The tribulation of poverty and wealth
• The tribulation of people with one another

text: Allah سبحانه وتعالى says:

text.text: (And We have made some of you [people] as trial for others - will you have patience?)
• Division and اختلاف (disagreement)

text:
This is among the greatest types of tribulations.
No one rejoices in tribulations except one in whom there is no khayr.

Rushing toward division and stirring it up indicates corruption of the inner self and absolute ignorance.
So beware of that, hold firmly to your religion, and be keen on uniting the word—this is from taqwā and from our aqidah.
Do not compromise your aqidah, whether you realize it or not.

Imām al-Ṭaḥāwī رحمه الله said in al-ʿAqīdah al-Ṭaḥāwiyyah:

text.text: “We see unity as truth and correctness, and division as deviation and punishment.”

text: Abū Dāwūd (4263) narrates from al-Miqdād ibn al-Aswad (رضي الله عنه) who said:

text.text:
By Allah, I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say:
“Indeed, the truly happy one is the one who is spared tribulations. Indeed, the truly happy one is the one who is spared tribulations. Indeed, the truly happy one is the one who is spared tribulations. And whoever is tested and remains patient—how excellent he is!”

text:
O Allah, we seek refuge in You from tribulations.
O Allah, keep us firm upon Your religion until we meet You.
O Allah, we seek refuge in You from everything Your Prophet Muḥammad ﷺ sought refuge from.

text.text: -Shaykha Umm 'Abdillāh Al-Wādiʿiyyah

text_entities.text: Seeking Refuge with Allah from Tribulations (Fitnah)

They said: We seek refuge with Allah from the tribulation of the Dajjāl.
رواه مسلم (2867)
this ḥadīth is the command to seek refuge from tribulations, both apparent and hidden.
“What is apparent and what is hidden”

means: what is done openly and what is concealed. It is also said: what occurs outwardly upon a person, and what exists in the heart such as shirk (polytheism), showing off (riyā’), envy, and other blameworthy inner thoughts.

• The tribulation of killing and fighting
Al-Bukhārī (447) narrates from Abū Saʿīd al-Khudrī رضي الله عنه:

We used to carry bricks one by one, while ʿAmmār carried two at a time. The Prophet ﷺ saw him, brushed the dust off him and said:

• The tribulation of illnesses and accidents
• The tribulation of losing loved ones and things dear to the soul
• The tribulation of children
• The tribulation of poverty and wealth
• The tribulation of people with one another
Allah سبحانه وتعالى says:
(And We have made some of you [people] as trial for others - will you have patience?)
• Division and اختلاف (disagreement)

This is among the greatest types of tribulations.
No one rejoices in tribulations except one in whom there is no khayr.

“We see unity as truth and correctness, and division as deviation and punishment.”
Abū Dāwūd (4263) narrates from al-Miqdād ibn al-Aswad (رضي الله عنه) who said:

By Allah, I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say:
“Indeed, the truly happy one is the one who is spared tribulations. Indeed, the truly happy one is the one who is spared tribulations. Indeed, the truly happy one is the one who is spared tribulations. And whoever is tested and remains patient—how excellent he is!”

O Allah, we seek refuge in You from tribulations.
O Allah, keep us firm upon Your religion until we meet You.
O Allah, we seek refuge in You from everything Your Prophet Muḥammad ﷺ sought refuge from.
-Shaykha Umm 'Abdillāh Al-Wādiʿiyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
messages.text:
Shaykh Yaḥyā al-Ḥajūrī حفظه الله said:

“My Lord, expand for me my chest, ease for me my affair, and untie the knot from my tongue so that they may understand my speech.”
He said: “You have been granted your request, O Mūsā” — meaning: it has been answered for you.
The Prophet of Allāh, Mūsā ﷺ, when Allāh عز وجل tasked him with conveying the message, Allāh inspired him with this supplication. This is because he ﷺ knew that when the chest becomes constricted, the tongue stumbles, and a person is unable to clearly express what he intends. Constriction of the chest prevents clarity of expression and proper articulation. Indeed, a constricted chest leads to confusion and to a lack of sound judgment in both speech and actions.

[عنوان المحاضرة: اضرار ضيق الصدر والقلق وعلاجها]

text_entities.text:
Shaykh Yaḥyā al-Ḥajūrī حفظه الله said:

“My Lord, expand for me my chest, ease for me my affair, and untie the knot from my tongue so that they may understand my speech.”
He said: “You have been granted your request, O Mūsā” — meaning: it has been answered for you.
The Prophet of Allāh, Mūsā ﷺ, when Allāh عز وجل tasked him with conveying the message, Allāh inspired him with this supplication. This is because he ﷺ knew that when the chest becomes constricted, the tongue stumbles, and a person is unable to clearly express what he intends. Constriction of the chest prevents clarity of expression and proper articulation. Indeed, a constricted chest leads to confusion and to a lack of sound judgment in both speech and actions.

[عنوان المحاضرة: اضرار ضيق الصدر والقلق وعلاجها]

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
messages.text:
وَلَقَدْ نَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ يَضِيقُ صَدْرُكَ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ ﴿٩٧﴾
"And We already know that your chest [I.e heart] is constricted by what they say" 

فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَكُنْ مِنَ السَّاجِدِينَ ﴿٩٨﴾
"So exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord, and be of those who prostrate [to Him]"

Al Hijr: 97-98

text_entities.text:
وَلَقَدْ نَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ يَضِيقُ صَدْرُكَ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ ﴿٩٧﴾
"And We already know that your chest [I.e heart] is constricted by what they say" 

فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَكُنْ مِنَ السَّاجِدِينَ ﴿٩٨﴾
"So exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord, and be of those who prostrate [to Him]"

Al Hijr: 97-98

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
messages.text:
Imām al-Saʿdī رحمه الله says in his tafsīr of verse 98 of Sūrat al-Ḥijr:

“So you, O Muḥammad, ‘exalt with praise of your Lord and be among those who prostrate’ — meaning: increase in the remembrance of Allāh, in glorifying Him, praising Him, and in prayer. For indeed, that expands and opens the chest and helps you in your affairs.”

text_entities.text:
Imām al-Saʿdī رحمه الله says in his tafsīr of verse 98 of Sūrat al-Ḥijr:

“So you, O Muḥammad, ‘exalt with praise of your Lord and be among those who prostrate’ — meaning: increase in the remembrance of Allāh, in glorifying Him, praising Him, and in prayer. For indeed, that expands and opens the chest and helps you in your affairs.”

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: The types of ʾAdhkār (rememberance) are four :
That which is bound to a specific time,

text:
such as: The morning and evening ʾadhkār.

2)

text.text: That which is bound to an act of worship,

text:
such as: The ʾadhkār which are after the ṣalāh.
This is indicated by the āyah where Allāh—Glorified and Exalted is He—says :

text.text: «And when you have completed the prayer, remember Allāh standing, sitting, or [lying] on your sides..» {Sūrat an-Nisāʾ : 103}
That which is bound to a reason

text:
, such as: The ʾAdhkār for entering the masjid and exiting the masjid.

When entering the masjid one should begin with their right foot, and say the basmallah (i.e Bismillāh), and send Ṣalawāt upon the Prophet ﷺ and say:  

«ʿA`ūdhu billāhil-ʿaẓīm, wa bi-wajhihil-karīm, wa sultānihil-qadīm, min ash-shaytānir-rajīm. Allāhummaftaḥ lī ʾabwāba raḥmatik.»

Translation : [I seek refuge in Almighty Allāh, by His Noble Face, by His Primordial power, from Satan the outcast. O Allāh, open before me the doors of Your mercy.]


And when leaving the masjid, begin with the left foot, and say : «Bismillāh waṣṣalātu wassalāmu ʿalā Rasūlillāh, Allāhumma ʾinnī ʾasʾaluka min faḍlik, Allāhumma ʿiṣimnī min ash-shaytānir-rajīm.»

Translation : [In the Name of Allāh, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allāh. O Allāh, I ask for Your favor, O Allāh, protect me from Satan the outcast.]

▫️He specified (doors) in the dhikr of entering the masjid

text.text: as the masjid is a place where Allah's mercy and grace decends.

text:
▫️He specificed (your favor) in the dhikr of exiting the masjid as it is required from the person to seek a livelihood. 

And from that is the saying of Allāh Almighty:

text.text: «And when the prayer has been concluded, disperse within the land and seek from the bounty of Allāh, and remember Allāh often that you may succeed.» {Sūrat Al-Jumʿah : 10}

text:
And likewise entering the bathroom and exiting it. It is said when entering : [Allāhumma ʾinnī ʾaʿūdhu bika minal-khubthi walkhabāʾith.]
Translation: [O Allāh, I seek protection in You from evil and the evil ones]

And when exiting : [Ghufrānaka]
Translation : [I seek Your forgiveness.]


4)

text.text: That which is not bound by a time, or obligatory act, or reason

text:
which is: General and unrestricted dhikr.

 And from that is the saying of the prophet ﷺ : 《Always keep your tongue moist with the remembrance of Allāh.》


Extracted from the live lesson «Explanaing the book Bulūgh al-Marām» for the Ustadh and Doctor Sāmī aṣ-Ṣaqīr (may Allāh grant him success) Member of the Council of Senior Scholars.

Tr: Umm Tamīm aṣ-Ṣūmāliyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: From the signs of the people of innovation:
Rebellion against the rulers.
by: Shaykhah Umm ʿAbdillāh al-Wādiʿiyyah —حفظها الله—

text:
:

Al-Dārimī narrated in his Sunan (no. 100) and al-Ājurrī in ash-Sharīʿah (no. 135) from Abū Qilābah, who said:

text.text: “No man introduces an innovation except that he deems the sword lawful.”

text:
﻿
This report is authentic—meaning rebellion against oppressive rulers.

This is among the consequences and evils of innovations.

text.text: No one rebels against the rulers except one who is upon innovation.

text:
As for the one who is upon the Sunnah, he is the furthest of people from rebelling against the rulers.

Everyone who rebels against an unjust ruler has within him a Khariji, Muʿtazili inclination—whether from the Shīʿah, the Ikhwān al-Muslimīn, or others. This method is among the most prominent reprehensible traits of the people of innovation: rebelling against oppressive rulers and inciting the people to do so. This is contrary to the Sharīʿah and contrary to the sound aqīdah.

Imām al-Ṭaḥāwī—رحمه الله—said in al-ʿAqīdah aṭ-Ṭaḥāwiyyah:

text.text:
“We do not hold the view of rebelling against our leaders and those in authority over us, even if they are unjust.
We do not supplicate against them, nor do we withdraw our hand from obedience to them.
We consider obedience to them to be obedience to Allāh, the Mighty and Majestic, an obligation, so long as they do not command disobedience.
And we supplicate for them for rectitude and well-being.”

text:
The Prophet ﷺ commanded listening and obedience to the ruler, warned against rebelling against him, commanded patience over his injustice, and commanded that the neck of the one who rebels against the ruler be struck.

Al-Bukhārī (no. 3793) narrated from Anas ibn Mālik رضي الله عنه that the Prophet ﷺ said to the Anṣār:

text.text: “You will see after me selfishness (i.e., leaders giving preference to themselves) and matters which you will disapprove of. so be patient until you meet me, for your appointment is at the Ḥawḍ (the basin).”

text: Imām Muslim narrated in his Ṣaḥīḥ (no. 1846) from Wāʾil ibn Ḥujr al-Ḥaḍramī رضي الله عنه that Salamah ibn Yazīd al-Juʿfī asked the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, saying:

text.text: "O Messenger of Allāh, what do you think if rulers rise over us who demand their rights from us but withhold our rights—what do you command us?”

text: He turned away from him. Then he asked him again, and he turned away. Then he asked him a second or third time, so al-Ashʿath ibn Qays pulled him and said:

text.text: “Listen and obey, for upon them is what they are burdened with, and upon you is what you are burdened with.”

text: The people of innovation are a source of misfortune for the servants and the lands—in their calling, their teaching, and their revolutions—by distancing people from holding firmly to their religion. And the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ spoke truth when he said:

text.text: “Indeed, the thing I fear most for my Ummah is the misguided leaders.”

text:
Narrated by Ibn Abī ʿĀṣim in as-Sunnah.

What occurs of revolutions, rebellion against the ruler, and igniting the fires of tribulation is due to ignorance of the correct aqīdah—the aqīdah of the righteous predecessors (as-salaf aṣ-ṣāliḥ).

text.text: Learning the aqīdah from its pure source is a fortress against tribulations, a source of safety and security, and a great happiness in this world and the next.
And this is something that is incumbent upon us:

text: to learn the aqīdah—the aqīdah of the prophets and messengers—so that we may attain Paradise and tread the path of salvation at a time when tribulations are like pieces of a dark night.

text.text: translation: umm ʿabdirraḥmān as-sūdāniyyah غفر الله لها

Rebellion against the rulers.
by: Shaykhah Umm ʿAbdillāh al-Wādiʿiyyah —حفظها الله—

:

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: To the Lonely, Saddened Hearts…

text:
" I feel lonely, misunderstood and hurt. ever since I started practicing the people I was once close with have distanced themselves, my family says hurtful words and no one tries to understand how I think or feel"

The Prophet ﷺ informed us of the reality of souls, how they get along and how they are repelled. 
He said:

text.text:
الأرواحُ جُنودٌ مُجَنَّدَةٌ، فما تَعارَفَ منها ائْتَلَفَ، وما تَناكَرَ منها اخْتَلَفَ
Souls are like gathered troops—those that recognize one another come together, and those that do not, differ.

text: And he ﷺ also said words whose meaning reminds us how rare truth truly is:

text.text:
ما أنتم في أهل الشرك إلا كالشعرة البيضاء في جلد الثور الأسود
"you are not [among the people of shirk] except like the white strand of hair in the skin of a black bull"
We are not the majority.

text: Considering how few the people of the Sunnah are, and how scattered they are across the world, it is only natural that they will clash with most people around them.

text.text: Especially

text:
when one takes the path of istiqāmah and knowledge at a young age.

So ask yourself honestly: do you really expect to be understood?

And even if people did understand you—what then? Would they react the way you hope? The way you expect? Not necessarily.

 People respond according to their own nature, their own desires, their own limitations. That is the nature of people. They are, by default, concerned with themselves.

text.text: They are not obligated to carry your emotional weight.

text:
But do you really need them to?

They hurt you. They misunderstood you. Your feelings were wounded. You felt emotionally shaken.
Then what?

Are you waiting for people to heal what they themselves caused?

text.text: Do you expect the cure to come from the same herb that poisoned you?

text:
That will never happen.

The Prophet ﷺ said:

text.text:
بَدَأَ الْإِسْلَامُ غَرِيبًا وَسَيَعُودُ غَرِيبًا فَطُوبَى لِلْغُرَبَاءِ

"Islam began as something strange and will return as something strange—so glad tidings to the strangers."

text:
The Prophet ﷺ called you a stranger and gave you glad tidings. So do you truly expect a stranger—rarer than diamonds—to be understood by the masses? Even diamonds are formed under pressure!

I have long said, and it is well known in meaning:
العلاقات مصالح

text.text: Relationships are built upon interests.

text:
Every relationship carries a certain maṣlaḥah. That benefit may differ from person to person. A relationship at school often ends when school ends. A relationship at work ends when the shift ends. Even relationships built upon love carry a form of benefit (as love in itself is a benefit)—and when that benefit fades, the relationship often fades with it.

So ask yourself:

text.text: what is your maṣlaḥah with those who hurt you?

text:
Take what is lawful, and leave the rest. You do not need more from them. You do not need them to understand you. Even if they did, they would likely not respond the way you want them to.

You were hurt—then what?

text.text: Life continues. People move on.

text:
So we deal with people only as Allah commanded us to.

Allah says:

text.text:
وَأَحْسِنُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
“And do good; indeed, Allah loves the doers of good.”

text: And the Prophet ﷺ said:

text.text:
إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَتَبَ الإِحْسَانَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ
“Indeed, Allah has prescribed excellence (Iḥsān) in all things.”

text:
Iḥsān is of two types. The first—and the greatest—is iḥsān toward Allah in worship, which is obligatory.

 The second is iḥsān toward people, some of which is obligatory and some recommended.

Shaykh Ibn ʿUthaymīn رحمه الله defined iḥsān toward people as:

"بذل الندى، وكف الأذى، وطلاقة الوجه"

To give what you are able to give, to withhold harm, and to meet people with a pleasant face.

The Prophet ﷺ said:

text.text:
المسلم من سلم المسلمون من لسانه ويده
“The Believer is the one from whose tongue and hand others are safe.”

text: And he ﷺ also said:

text.text:
لا تحقرن من المعروف شيئًا ولو أن تلقى أخاك بوجه طلق
“Do not belittle any good deed, even meeting your brother with a cheerful face.”

text:
So you fulfilled iḥsān. You refrained from harm. You showed good character. You earned reward.

And that is your maṣlaḥah with people.

“The Believer is the one from whose tongue and hand others are safe.”
And he ﷺ also said:

لا تحقرن من المعروف شيئًا ولو أن تلقى أخاك بوجه طلق
“Do not belittle any good deed, even meeting your brother with a cheerful face.”

So you fulfilled iḥsān. You refrained from harm. You showed good character. You earned reward.

And that is your maṣlaḥah with people.

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
Your reward with Allah, to do what Allah commanded you with towards them and gain the reward for it.

Reflect deeply when you say:

text.text: حَسْبِيَ اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيل

text: What does

text.text: ḥasbiyallāh

text:
mean?

It means

text.text: Allah is sufficient for me.

text: Allah is enough for me against everything. Enough for me against all creation.

text.text: I am in need of no one if Allah is with me.

text: والله ما خاب من كان الله معه

text.text: By Allah, the one who has Allah with him is never lost.

text:
What do you need if Allah is with you?

The Prophet ﷺ said to Ibn ʿAbbās رضي الله عنهما:

text.text:
واعلم أنَّ الأُمّة لَو اِجتَمَعَت عَلَىٰ أن يَنفَعُوكَ بِشَيءٍ لَنْ يَنْفَعُوكَ إِلَّا بِشَيْءٍ قَدْ كَتَبَهُ اللَّهُ لَكَ، وَلَو اِجتَمَعُوا عَلَىٰ أن يَضُرُّوكَ بِشَيءٍ لَنْ يَضُرُّوكَ إِلَّا بِشَيْءٍ قَدْ كَتَبَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ
“And know that if the entire ummah were to gather together to benefit you with something, they would not benefit you except with something that Allah has already written for you. And if they were to gather together to harm you with something, they would not harm you except with something that Allah has already written against you.”
People can do nothing except carry out what Allah has already decreed for you.

text:
That is it.

Do not depend on people.

Yes, it hurts. And pain is real. Many have walked this path and felt this weight.

text.text: But Allah is enough.

text:
If you want peace in a world full of deficiency—then let Allāh be enough for you.

You want provision?

text.text: Allāh is Ar-Razzāq.

text: You want mercy?

text.text: Allāh is Ar-Raḥmān, Ar-Raḥīm.

text: Your heart is broken?

text.text: Allāh is Al-Jabbār.

text: You want to be heard?

text.text: Allāh is As-Samīʿ.

text: You want an answer?

text.text: Allāh is Al-Mujīb.

text: You want a spouse, children, gifts?

text.text: Allāh is Al-Wahhāb.

text:
What more do you want?

You were not created for this world.

text.text: You are meant to feel estranged here.

text:
You belong in Jannah إن شاء الله.

Allāh describes it:

text.text:
﴿وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ نَاعِمَةٌ ۝ لِسَعْيِهَا رَاضِيَةٌ ۝ فِي جَنَّةٍ عَالِيَةٍ  ۝ لَا تَسمَعُ فِيهَا لَاغِيةً  ۝ فِيهَا عَيْنٌ جَارِيَةٌ  ۝ فِيهَا سُرُرٌ مَّرْفُوعَةٌ  ۝ وَأَكْوَابٌ مَّوْضُوعَةٌ  ۝ وَنَمَارِقُ مَصْفُوفَةٌ  ۝ وَزَرَابِيُّ مَبْثُوثَةٌ ﴾
" [other] faces that day will show pleasure, with their effort [they are] satisfied, in an elevated garden, wherein they will hear no unsuitable speech, within it is a flowing spring, within it are couches raised high, and cups put in place, and cushions lined up, and carpets spread around"

text: [Al-Ghāshiyah: 8-16]

text.text: That

text:
is where you belong—by Allah’s permission.

And then Allah says:

text.text:
{أَفَلَا يَنظُرُونَ إِلَى الْإِبِلِ كَيْفَ خُلِقَتْ  ۝ وَإِلَى السَّمَاءِ كَيْفَ رُفِعَتْ  ۝ وَإِلَى الْجِبَالِ كَيْفَ نُصِبَتْ  ۝ وَإِلَى الْأَرْضِ كَيْفَ سُطِحَتْ}
" Then do they not look at the camels – how they are created? And at the sky – how it is raised? And at the mountains – how they are erected? And at the earth – how it is spread out?"

text:
[Al-Ghāshiyah: 17-20]

The One who created all of this—

text.text: will He not be enough for you?

text:
Let Allah be sufficient for you.

Wallāhi, you do not need people.

You need Allah.

And though you may find moments of comfort with people—

text.text: true solace is with Allah.

text:
The salaf used to say:
لو علم الملوك وأبناء الملوك ما نحن فيه من النعيم لجالدونا عليه بالسيوف

"If the kings and the sons of kings knew the sweetness we are in, they would fight us for it with swords."

text.text: let your only concern be how to please the Lord of the worlds.

text: Rely on Him and He will suffice you.

text.text:
{وَمَنْ يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ}
"And whoever relies upon Allah — then He is sufficient for him" [At-Talāq: 3]

- كتبته أُمُّ عَبدِالرَّحمـٰن السُّودَانِيّة — غفر الله لها 
٢٣ رجب ١٤٤٧هـ

text: Shaykh Ibn Baz رحمه الله said:

text.text: "With calamities comes a call to return to Allah and a warning for the people, so that they may return to Him."

text: (Majmū' Fatāwa wa Maqālāt, 5/175)

text_entities.text: Shaykh Ibn Baz رحمه الله said:
"With calamities comes a call to return to Allah and a warning for the people, so that they may return to Him."
(Majmū' Fatāwa wa Maqālāt, 5/175)

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text: Sufyan ath-Thawrī رحمه الله said:

text.text: "The servant will not taste the sweetness of īmān until trials come to him from every place."

text: Hilyatul Awliyāh 10/204

text_entities.text: Sufyan ath-Thawrī رحمه الله said:
"The servant will not taste the sweetness of īmān until trials come to him from every place."
Hilyatul Awliyāh 10/204

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text: Anas ibn Mālik رضي الله عنه reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:

text.text: “Indeed, the Ḥūr al-ʿĪn will sing in Paradise, saying: ‘We are the beautiful Ḥūr, preserved for noble husbands.’”

text: authenticated by Al-Albāni

text_entities.text: Anas ibn Mālik رضي الله عنه reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
“Indeed, the Ḥūr al-ʿĪn will sing in Paradise, saying: ‘We are the beautiful Ḥūr, preserved for noble husbands.’”
authenticated by Al-Albāni

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text: {وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا وَمَسَاكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِي جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ ۚ وَرِضْوَانٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ}

text.text: “Allāh has promised the believing men and believing women gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they will abide eternally, and pleasant dwellings in Gardens of ‘adn. And the greatest [blessing] is the pleasure of Allāh—that is the supreme success.”

text: [at-Tawbah: 72]

text.text: Ibn Kathīr –may Allāh have mercy on him– said in his Tafsīr:

text: “That is, Allāh’s being pleased with them is greater, nobler, and more magnificent than the bliss they are in. As Imām Mālik narrated from Zayd ibn Aslam, from ʿAṭāʾ ibn Yasār, from Abū Saʿīd al-Khudrī, that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said:

text.text: “Indeed, Allāh, the Mighty and Majestic, will say to the people of Paradise: O people of Paradise! They will say: At Your service, our Lord, and all goodness is in Your Hands. He will say: Are you pleased? They will say: And how could we not be pleased, O Lord, when You have given us what You have not given to anyone of Your creation? He will say: Shall I not give you something better than that? They will say: O Lord, what could be better than this? He will say: I bestow upon you My pleasure, and I will never be displeased with you thereafter, ever.”

text: This hadith is agreed upon.

text_entities.text: {وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا وَمَسَاكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِي جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ ۚ وَرِضْوَانٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ}

“Allāh has promised the believing men and believing women gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they will abide eternally, and pleasant dwellings in Gardens of ‘adn. And the greatest [blessing] is the pleasure of Allāh—that is the supreme success.”
[at-Tawbah: 72]
Ibn Kathīr –may Allāh have mercy on him– said in his Tafsīr:

“That is, Allāh’s being pleased with them is greater, nobler, and more magnificent than the bliss they are in. As Imām Mālik narrated from Zayd ibn Aslam, from ʿAṭāʾ ibn Yasār, from Abū Saʿīd al-Khudrī, that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said:

“Indeed, Allāh, the Mighty and Majestic, will say to the people of Paradise: O people of Paradise! They will say: At Your service, our Lord, and all goodness is in Your Hands. He will say: Are you pleased? They will say: And how could we not be pleased, O Lord, when You have given us what You have not given to anyone of Your creation? He will say: Shall I not give you something better than that? They will say: O Lord, what could be better than this? He will say: I bestow upon you My pleasure, and I will never be displeased with you thereafter, ever.”
This hadith is agreed upon.

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: Ibn al-Qayyim رحمه الله said:

text: “Sin inevitably brings humiliation. True honor — all of it — lies in obedience to Allah. Allah the Exalted said:

text.text: {Whoever desires honor — then to Allah belongs all honor} [Fāṭir: 10],
meaning:

text:
Let him seek it through obedience to Allah, for he will not find it except in Allah’s obedience.

Some of the Salaf would make this duʿāʾ:

“O Allah, honor me through Your obedience, and do not humiliate me through disobedience to You.”

text.text: Al-Ḥasan al-Baṣrī رحمه الله said:

text: "Even if they ride fine mules and well-groomed horses, the humiliation of sin never leaves their hearts. Allah refuses except to humiliate those who disobey Him.”

text.text: And ʿAbdullāh ibn al-Mubārak said:

text:
رأيتُ الذنوبَ تُميتُ القلوبَ
I saw that sins cause the death of hearts,

وقد يُورِثُ الذُّلَّ إدمانُها
And persisting in them may lead to humiliation.

وتركُ الذنوبِ حياةُ القلوبِ
Abandoning sins gives life to the hearts,

وخيرٌ لنفسِك عِصيانُها
And disobeying the self (from sin) is better for it.

text.text: [Ad-Dāʾ wa ad-Dawāʾ, p. 69]

text_entities.text: Ibn al-Qayyim رحمه الله said:

{Whoever desires honor — then to Allah belongs all honor} [Fāṭir: 10],
meaning:

Let him seek it through obedience to Allah, for he will not find it except in Allah’s obedience.

Some of the Salaf would make this duʿāʾ:

“O Allah, honor me through Your obedience, and do not humiliate me through disobedience to You.”
Al-Ḥasan al-Baṣrī رحمه الله said:
"Even if they ride fine mules and well-groomed horses, the humiliation of sin never leaves their hearts. Allah refuses except to humiliate those who disobey Him.”
And ʿAbdullāh ibn al-Mubārak said:

رأيتُ الذنوبَ تُميتُ القلوبَ

وقد يُورِثُ الذُّلَّ إدمانُها

وتركُ الذنوبِ حياةُ القلوبِ

[Ad-Dāʾ wa ad-Dawāʾ, p. 69]

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: Sins Strip Away Hayā'

text:
Among the punishments of sins is the loss of ḥayāʾ, which is the lifeblood of the heart and the foundation of all goodness. When it disappears, all goodness vanishes with it.

In Ṣaḥīḥ al-Bukhārī, it is reported that the Prophet ﷺ said:

text.text: “Modesty is all good.”

text: And he said:

text.text: “From what the people have retained from the words of the earliest prophethood is: If you feel no shame, then do as you wish.”

text: This last statement has two interpretations:

text.text: First interpretation:

text: It is a warning and threat—its meaning is: whoever feels no shame will proceed to do whatever vile deeds he wishes, because it is modesty that restrains him from such acts. If modesty is absent and nothing holds him back, he will fall into them. This is the interpretation of Abū ʿUbayd.

text.text: Second interpretation:

text:
It means: if an action carries no shame before Allah, then go ahead and do it. What should be avoided is only that which brings shame before Allah. This is the interpretation of Imām Aḥmad as narrated by Ibn Hāniʾ.

According to the first view, it is a threat—like the verse:

text.text: “Do as you will.” [Fussilat: 40]

text:
According to the second view, it is permission or approval.

Now, if one asks: Is it possible to reconcile both meanings and interpret the statement as containing both?

text.text: I say:

text: No, not even according to those who allow interpreting shared-word meanings with all their definitions, because of the contradiction between permission and threat. However, considering one of the meanings naturally leads to recognizing the other.

text.text: The point is:

text: sins weaken hayāʾ in the servant—until it may be completely stripped away. A person might reach the point where he is unaffected by others knowing of his sins or being exposed. Many even speak openly about their wickedness and the vile acts they commit—and what drives them to this is their loss of modesty.

text.text: When someone reaches this state, there remains no hope for his reform.
[Ad-Dāʾ wa ad-Dawāʾ, p. 81]

text_entities.text: Sins Strip Away Hayā'

Among the punishments of sins is the loss of ḥayāʾ, which is the lifeblood of the heart and the foundation of all goodness. When it disappears, all goodness vanishes with it.

In Ṣaḥīḥ al-Bukhārī, it is reported that the Prophet ﷺ said:
“Modesty is all good.”
And he said:
“From what the people have retained from the words of the earliest prophethood is: If you feel no shame, then do as you wish.”
This last statement has two interpretations:
First interpretation:

It is a warning and threat—its meaning is: whoever feels no shame will proceed to do whatever vile deeds he wishes, because it is modesty that restrains him from such acts. If modesty is absent and nothing holds him back, he will fall into them. This is the interpretation of Abū ʿUbayd.
Second interpretation:

It means: if an action carries no shame before Allah, then go ahead and do it. What should be avoided is only that which brings shame before Allah. This is the interpretation of Imām Aḥmad as narrated by Ibn Hāniʾ.

According to the first view, it is a threat—like the verse:
“Do as you will.” [Fussilat: 40]

According to the second view, it is permission or approval.

Now, if one asks: Is it possible to reconcile both meanings and interpret the statement as containing both?
I say:

No, not even according to those who allow interpreting shared-word meanings with all their definitions, because of the contradiction between permission and threat. However, considering one of the meanings naturally leads to recognizing the other.
The point is:

sins weaken hayāʾ in the servant—until it may be completely stripped away. A person might reach the point where he is unaffected by others knowing of his sins or being exposed. Many even speak openly about their wickedness and the vile acts they commit—and what drives them to this is their loss of modesty.
When someone reaches this state, there remains no hope for his reform.
[Ad-Dāʾ wa ad-Dawāʾ, p. 81]

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text: بسم الله الرحمـٰن الرحيم

text.text:
من تزوج امرأة تلبس النقاب والقفاز ولا تتفرج على التلفاز وإلى أهل الحق تنحاز ولا تذهب إلى الحمام ولا تكثر الملام فقد حاز على امرأة الأحلام
 [الإمام الألمعي ص٢٤٨]

text:
Whoever marries a woman who wears a niqab and gloves, doesn't watch television, sides with the righteous, doesn't go to the hammām (public saunas/pools), and doesn't complain excessively, has found the woman of dreams.
[Al-imām Al-Almaʿi p.248]

A very widespread quote attributed to imām Al-wādiʿi رحمه الله.

When I opened the book Al-imām Al-Almaʿi and went through it I did not find a single line of this quote mentioned, and in page 248 the only thing he said about women is:

"النِّساء السّلفيات أغلى من الذهب الأحمر"
"Salafi women are more precious than red gold"

but that's it.

I tried to find another source for this quote or proof that he said it but none at all. 

I asked Shaykha Umm Salama wife of Shaykh Muqbil if he really said that and this was her response:

"Shaykh al-Wādiʿī was not someone who used rhymed, ornate speech or embellished his words. So I do not think this is his speech at all. And since you searched in the book and did not find it, then it would be something falsely attributed to him."

This really highlights the importance of mentioning and checking the sources of whatever we share or read.

It is important that when we quote someone we make sure it is not falsely attributed to them, and when we do know who the quote belongs to then it is important we mention so and this is from الأمانة العِلمية (ʿilm integrity) and is how our deen is preserved.

Imām Muḥammad bin sīrīn said:
"إِنَّ هَذَا الْعِلْمَ دِينٌ. فَانْظُرُوا عَمَّنْ تَأْخُذُونَ دِينَكُمْ."
"Indeed this knowledge is religion, so look to whom you take your religion from."
[Saḥiḥ Muslim 1/14]

And the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 
 "كفى بِالْمَرْءِ كَذِبًا أَنْ يُحَدِّثَ بِكُلِّ مَا سَمِعَ".
“It is enough of a lie for a person to repeat everything he hears.”
[Saḥiḥ Muslim 1/10]

So fear Allāh in what you share, especially during a time when tribulations are like pieces of a dark night, when truth and falsehood have mixed we must strive to seek the truth and differentiate it. 

كتبته أُمُّ عَبدِالرَّحمـٰن السُّودَانِيّة غفر الله لها
ليلة ٢ شعبان ١٤٤٧هـ

text_entities.text: بسم الله الرحمـٰن الرحيم

من تزوج امرأة تلبس النقاب والقفاز ولا تتفرج على التلفاز وإلى أهل الحق تنحاز ولا تذهب إلى الحمام ولا تكثر الملام فقد حاز على امرأة الأحلام
 [الإمام الألمعي ص٢٤٨]

Whoever marries a woman who wears a niqab and gloves, doesn't watch television, sides with the righteous, doesn't go to the hammām (public saunas/pools), and doesn't complain excessively, has found the woman of dreams.
[Al-imām Al-Almaʿi p.248]

A very widespread quote attributed to imām Al-wādiʿi رحمه الله.

When I opened the book Al-imām Al-Almaʿi and went through it I did not find a single line of this quote mentioned, and in page 248 the only thing he said about women is:

"النِّساء السّلفيات أغلى من الذهب الأحمر"
"Salafi women are more precious than red gold"

but that's it.

I tried to find another source for this quote or proof that he said it but none at all.

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: Shaykh Rabī' Al-Madkhali رحمه الله said:

text:
As for what is called “religious nasheeds,” then these—of course—the Salaf did not have such nasheeds.

The Salaf had ḥidāʾ (travel chants), and they had poetry that served Islam, and the way they recited it and spoke with it was different from the method of nasheeds.

This method that is used today in nasheeds is taken from the Christians, taken from the Rāfiḍah, and taken from the Ṣūfiyyah.
These nasheeds were adopted by some groups, and they became a foundational principle from their foundations, and they are unable to give them up, no matter how much the scholars speak and clarify.

Aḥmad and ash-Shāfiʿī and those like them fought against these nasheeds, and they used to call them at that time “at-Taghbīr”, meaning religious chants, meaning that they stir religious emotions.

Ash-Shāfiʿī said:

text.text: “This was invented by the heretics in order to divert people away from the Book of Allah.”

text:
And the one who becomes infatuated with nasheeds—his resolve and his desire for the Qurʾān becomes weak.

We have already experienced this and come to know people from these categories.

[Marḥaban yā ṭālib Al 'ilm p. 348]

text.text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: The Glory of the Qurān and Its Prestige

text:
Indeed the greatness of the Qurān, its prestige, and its glory has an impact on souls, and its affect on the heart and limbs is a sign of its miraculous nature, its particular manner, and the power of its meanings.

It is no wonder for the Great Qurān, it is great in its meanings and manner, majestic in its topics and purposes, its benefits and effects widespread. 

Allāh Almighty says:

text.text: "If We had sent down this Qur’ān upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled and splitting from fear of Allāh. And these examples We present to the people that perhaps they will give thought." [Sūrat Al-Ḥashr: 21]

text:
The Ḥāfidh Ibn Kathīr رحمه الله in his tafsīr says: "The Almighty says—greatifying the matter of the Qurān and clarifying its high status—that the hearts ought to tremble for it, and crack upon hearing it because of the true promise and certain warning it contains. 

"If

We had sent down this Qur’ān upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled and splitting from fear of Allāh. And these examples We present to the people"
Meaning: If a mountain, in its thickness and hardness, were to understand this Qurʾān and contemplate what is in it and would humble itself and crack apart from the fear of Allāh, Glorified and Exalted. ​So how is it fitting for you, O humans, that your hearts do not soften, tremble, and crack from the fear of Allāh, when you have understood Allāh's command and contemplated His Book?

 For this reason, the Almighty said: "And these examples We present to the people that perhaps they will give thought."

If deaf mountains had they heard the words of Allāh and understood them, would have trembled and cracked from the fear of Him. So what about you, when you have heard and understood? 

📖 The State of the Salaf with the Qurʾān p.30 | حال السلف مع القرآن ص٣٠
Tr : Umm Tamīm aṣ-Ṣūmāliyyah

text: Shaykha Umm ʿAbdillāh Al-wādiʿiyyah —حفظها الله— says:

text.text: From the advice of some of my virtuous sisters to me:

text:
One of my sisters — may Allah preserve her — when she visited us from the Emirates, about five years before the death of my father, Shaykh Muqbil bin Hadi Al-Wadi'i, may Allah have mercy on him, she visited Dar Al-Hadith in Dammaj. We sat with her, and she mentioned a story: that Sa’eed ibn Al-Musayyib, when he wanted to leave his wife’s place to seek ḥadīth, she asked him: “Where are you going?” He said: “To Abu Hurayrah.” She said: “Sit down, for Abu Hurayrah's knowledge is with me.”

Sa’eed ibn Al-Musayyib was married to Abu Hurayrah’s daughter, may Allah be pleased with him. So she advised me: “Be like this — have your father’s knowledge with you!”

Some advice is very precious and valuable. Whoever does not benefit from it or neglects it will surely regret it and remember it with a broken heart full of regret and sorrow.

فلقدْ نصحتُكَ إنْ قبلتَ نصيحتي... فالنُّصحُ أغلى ما يُباعُ ويُوهَبُ
So I have advised you, if you accept my advice — For sincere advice is the most precious thing one can give or receive.

May Allah benefit us with such valuable advice.

taken from:

text.text: Shaykha Umm ʿAbdillāh's blog
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

text.text: A Piece Of Advice regarding the issue of taswīr

text:
Go easy on common folks regarding the issue if tasweer.

There's a lot of falsehood out there, and the whole world has no issue with using and making pictures.

It takes a considerable amount of courage to go against all of that, and join the isolated island of truth.

If Allāh has not granted you tawfeeq and guidance, you'd be out there sharing memes and watching funny videos.

It's quite heavy to accept that tasweer of animate beings is ḥarām, so do your best to make your advice and approach as tolerable and easy as you could.

Pretend you want to keep a thin cord attached between you and them. They're already pulling so hard, so don't mirror that. Go soft. (Wisdom benefited from a narration from Mu’āwiyah, may Allāh be pleased with him. Not sure if authentic, but the meaning is correct).

With that said, I feel optimistic, that this scourge will soon meet its demise, which is why I named my book what means: The Rising of the Dawn's Light, Upon the Issue of Photography in Its Darkest Night.

Meaning, inshaa'Allah, this book is the start of fighting the darkness of this issue, and raising awareness about it.

والله المستعان.

---

text.text: You think that you will not be punished for your sin,

text: وأنت حرمت قيام الليل

text.text: And you were deprived of praying at night

text:
…


و وردك من القرآن

text.text: and from reciting your (regular) share from Qurʾān

text: حرمت السنن الرواتب

text.text: and deprived of the sunan ar-rawātib

text: وانعقد لسانك عن ذكر الله

text.text: And your tongue has become bound from the remembrance of Allah.

text: طبع على قلبك شيئا فشيئا وأنت لا تشعر وتظن أنك لا تعاقب لأنك لست مبتلى في دنياك

text.text: Your heart was sealed little by little while you did not even feel it, and you think that you are not being punished because you are not afflicted in this world.

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
بسم الله الرحمـٰن الرحيم 

As Imām Muḥammad bin sīrīn رحمه الله said:

text.text:
"إِنَّ هَذَا الْعِلْمَ دِينٌ. فَانْظُرُوا عَمَّنْ تَأْخُذُونَ دِينَكُمْ."
"Indeed this knowledge is religion, so look to whom you take your religion from."
[Saḥiḥ Muslim 1/14]

text:
In light of sincere advice, we clarify the manhaj of this channel.

We follow the Qurʾān and Sunnah, according to the understanding of as-Salaf aṣ-Ṣāliḥ.

text.text: We condemn and detest:

text:
• Taʿaṣṣub/ Tamadh-hub [Blind adherence to a particular madhhab or individual] 

• Taḥazzub [Partisanship]

• Tamayyuʿ [watering down the religion]

• Ghuluw [extremism] in tabdīʿ, takfīr or tafsīq. 

• Bidaʿ [innovations] whether in ʿaqīdah, worship or manhaj.

• Shirk in all it's forms. 

•Maʿāṣī, both public and private.

This includes widespread evils such as free-mixing, tabarruj, music, taṣwīr, and publicizing or justifying sin.

We uphold clarity with justice, firmness without oppression, and refutation without falsehood, lying, or backbiting in the name of calling to the truth. We follow the ḥaqq wherever it is — and ḥaqq is not restricted to particular individuals or groups. 

but as Allah ﷻ said:

text.text:
ۖ فَإِن تَنَـٰزَعْتُمْ فِى شَىْءٍۢ فَرُدُّوهُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَٱلرَّسُولِ إِن كُنتُمْ تُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌۭ وَأَحْسَنُ تَأْوِيلًا 
"(And) if you differ in anything amongst yourselves, refer it to Allāh and His Messenger ﷺ, if you believe in Allāh and in the Last Day. That is better and more suitable for final determination." [An-Nisāʾ: 59]

text: (translation by Al-Hilāli & Khān رحمهم الله)

text.text:
It is narrated from al-ʿIrbāḍ ibn Sāriyah رضي الله عنه that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:

"فإنَّه مَن يَعِشْ منكم فسيَرَى اختِلافًا كثيرًا، فعليكم بسُنَّتي وسُنَّةِ الخُلَفاءِ الرَّاشِدينَ المَهْدِيِّينَ، عَضُّوا عليها بالنَّواجِذِ، وإيَّاكم ومُحدَثاتِ الأُمورِ؛ فإنَّ كُلَّ بِدعةٍ ضَلالةٌ"
“Indeed, whoever among you lives long will see much disagreement. So upon you is to adhere to my Sunnah and the Sunnah of the Rightly Guided Caliphs, Cling to it firmly with your molar teeth, and beware of newly invented matters, for indeed every innovation is misguidance.”
Narrated by at-Tirmidhī (2676).

text:
We ask Allah ﷻ to guide us and you to that which He loves and show us the truth as truth and allow us to follow it, and show us falsehood as falsehood and allow us to avoid it.

For advice or corrections please contact through forms:

text.text: https://forms.gle/K2hiGVfjVvKRTDWH8

text: وجزاكم الله خيرًا

It is narrated from al-ʿIrbāḍ ibn Sāriyah رضي الله عنه that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:

"فإنَّه مَن يَعِشْ منكم فسيَرَى اختِلافًا كثيرًا، فعليكم بسُنَّتي وسُنَّةِ الخُلَفاءِ الرَّاشِدينَ المَهْدِيِّينَ، عَضُّوا عليها بالنَّواجِذِ، وإيَّاكم ومُحدَثاتِ الأُمورِ؛ فإنَّ كُلَّ بِدعةٍ ضَلالةٌ"
“Indeed, whoever among you lives long will see much disagreement. So upon you is to adhere to my Sunnah and the Sunnah of the Rightly Guided Caliphs, Cling to it firmly with your molar teeth, and beware of newly invented matters, for indeed every innovation is misguidance.”
Narrated by at-Tirmidhī (2676).

We ask Allah ﷻ to guide us and you to that which He loves and show us the truth as truth and allow us to follow it, and show us falsehood as falsehood and allow us to avoid it.

For advice or corrections please contact through forms:
https://forms.gle/K2hiGVfjVvKRTDWH8
وجزاكم الله خيرًا

text: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم

text.text: RAMADAN PLANNERS & ADVENT CALENDARS

text:
The Prophet ﷺ did not teach us to do this, therefore it is not a Sunnah.

When we look to the Sahaba رضي الله عنهم, we find that they did not practice this, nor did the generations after them, such as the Tabi‘een رحمهم الله. This shows that it is not the way of those who preceded us.

Furthermore, this practice is not found in any of the books of fiqh.

A bid‘ah is an innovation that is introduced into the Deen without precedent and is connected to an act of ‘ibaadah.
Based on this, it is considered a bid‘ah.

text.text:
عن ام المؤمنين عائشة رضي الله عنها قالت 
قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم 
((من أحدث فى أمرنا هذا ما ليس منه فهو رد)) 

[Al-Bukhari 2697 Muslim 1718]

text:
الله المستعان

The danger of bid‘ah is that it grows over time, becoming more widespread and increasingly harmful. For this reason, it must not be enabled.

text.text: شيخة ام عبدالله شهيدة البريطانية (الله يحفظها)

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text: قال ابن القيم في طريق الهجرتين (ص٦٣):

text.text: فالسعيد الرابح من عامل الله فيهم ولم يعاملهم في الله ، وخاف الله فيهم ولم يخفهم في الله وأرضى الله بسخطهم ولم يرضهم بسخط الله، وراقب الله فيهم ولم يراقبهم في الله، وأثر الله عليهم ولم يؤثرهم فى الله، وأمات خوفهم ورجاءهم وحبهم من قلبه وأحى حب الله وخوفه ورجاءَه فيه، فهذا هو الذي يكتب عليهم، وتكون معاملته لهم كلها ربحاً، بشرط أن يصبر على أذاهم ويتخذه مغنماً لا مغرماً وربحاً لا خسراناً.

Ibn al-Qayyim said in Ṭarīq al-Hijratayn (p. 63):

The truly happy and successful one is he who deals with Allah concerning the people, and does not deal with the people concerning Allah; who fears Allah with regard to them, and does not fear them with regard to Allah; who pleases Allah even if it angers them, and does not please them at the cost of Allah’s displeasure; who observes Allah concerning them, and does not observe them concerning Allah; who gives precedence to Allah over them, and does not give them precedence over Allah. He causes fear of them, hope in them, and love of them to die in his heart, and gives life to it by love of Allah, fear of Him, and hope in Him. This is the one upon whom they are written (i.e., decreed, meaning: Allah grants him acceptance, influence, and decree over their hearts), and whose dealings with them are entirely profitable—on the condition that he is patient with their harm and takes it as a gain, not a loss, and as profit, not defeat.

text_entities.text: قال ابن القيم في طريق الهجرتين (ص٦٣):

text.text: What should someone who has days to make up and is unsure of the number do?

text:
The female questioner (أ ، ع) says : A woman has days to make up from Ramadān, but she is unsure if it is four days or three, she has now fasted three days; what is required of her?

He—may Allāh have mercy on him—answered : "If the person is in doubt regarding what he must make up, they should go with the lesser amount. If the woman or man is unsure wether they must make up three days or four, they should take the lesser amount; this is because the lesser amount is certain, whereas beyond that is doubted. The fundamental principle is for the person to be free of liability (Barā'at al-Dhimmah). However, despite that, it is safest to fast this day which was doubted; because if it was indeed obligatory then they will have cleared their liability with certainty, and if it was not obligatory then it is considered voluntary. And Allāh—Exalted is He—does not let the reward of those who do the best deeds be lost."

[Fatāwa Nūr 'Ala al-Darb 
Ibn Uthaymēn (7/304)]

text.text: Tr : umm tamīm as-sūmāliyyah

If the woman or man is unsure wether they must make up three days or four, they should take the lesser amount; this is because the lesser amount is certain, whereas beyond that is doubted. However, despite that, it is safest to fast this day which was doubted; because if it was indeed obligatory then they will have cleared their liability with certainty, and if it was not obligatory then it is considered voluntary. And Allāh—Exalted is He—does not let the reward of those who do the best deeds be lost."

[Fatāwa Nūr 'Ala al-Darb 
Ibn Uthaymēn (7/304)]

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: ليس كل العلم ينشر | Not every knowledge should be spread

text: When we share knowledge (of our dīn) we must look at the harms and benefits that will come about—

text.text: and this requires more knowledge

text:
.

Some masāʾil should be left from speaking about to the public. It is from ʿilm that you look to who you are speaking and addressing. 

As an example, praying in shoes is from the Sunnah. However, mentioning this to the general public without ḥikmah may lead to harm. People may begin praying in the masjid with dirty shoes, which could cause others to be repelled from coming to the masjid and say, “They claim to follow the Sunnah, yet they dirty the house of Allāh.”

There is a genuine concern when it comes to spreading knowledge and fear of concealing it [

text.text: Sūrah al-Baqarah:159

text:
]; but the ʿulemāʾ placed a criterion on spreading knowledge—between not spreading every bit of it and not withholding all of it. 

In Islām, prevention of corruption is given precedence over bringing benefit to prevent corruption being spread out by way of this (even if there is benefit in sharing it). 

Ash-Shāṭibī رحمه الله said: “Not every knowledge should be spread/broadcasted. Even if it is true.” 

He also added that Imām Mālik mentioned that he has many aḥadīth which he did not speak about nor did he relay it to anyone else—this is because he disliked any speech with no action that comes from it.
“وكان يكره الكلام فيما ليس تحته عمل"
[الموافقات ١٧١:٥]

The criterion the ʿulemāʾ say to use is to present the issue to the Shari’ah. If it is sound according to its scale, then look at its context regarding the state and era of the people—this requires ʿilm and ḥikmah; wisdom which can only be obtained by experience.

There is also the issue of

text.text: how

text: the knowledge is shared—the

text.text: way

text: in which it is conveyed; which is another discussion.

text.text:
An excerpt from my notes of  الطريق إلى النبوغ العلمي taught by Ustādh Abū Hudhayfa Sāmī حفظه الله
 ~ أم مصعب الغانية الألمانية
