messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
بسم الله الرحمـٰن الرحيم 

يقول الشاعر: 

اذا غامرتَ في شرفٍ مرومِ
فلا تقنعْ بما دونَ النجومِ
فطعمُ الموتِ في أمرٍ صغيرٍ
كطعمِ الموتِ في أمرٍ عظيمِ

"If you dare to pursue a lofty honor,
Then do not be content with less than the stars.
For the taste of death for the sake of a small matter
Is like the taste of death for the sake of a great matter."

whatever you set out to seek, whatever you make your goal, then strive for it fully. Aim high. For you will endure hardship regardless.

Every path in life contains difficulty. No one escapes struggle. But there are different kinds of struggle, and not all hardships are equal.

When you choose a path of disobedience to Allah ﷻ, that itself is a hardship. It burdens the heart, darkens the soul, and leads to regret — even if it appears easy in the moment.

And when you choose to obey Allah, to hold firmly to the Qur’an and the Sunnah, that too is a hardship. It requires patience, restraint, and steadfastness.

But the difference is that one hardship leads to loss, And the other leads to salvation.

And surely people will not make the latter easy for you.

They will call you extreme. They will say you are too harsh on yourself. They will tell you to “relax,” to “be moderate,” [despite not understanding what moderation even is] to “stop making the religion difficult.”

And facing this pressure is not easy — especially when it comes from those closest to you.

Allah ﷻ told us in the Qurʾān: 

أَحَسِبَ النَّاسُ أَنْ يُتْرَكُوا أَنْ يَقُولُوا آمَنَّا وَهُمْ لَا يُفْتَنُونَ ○ وَلَقَدْ فَتَنَّا الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ۖ فَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ الْكَاذِبِينَ

“Do people think that they will be left to say, ‘We believe,’ and they will not be tested? And We certainly tested those before them, so Allah will surely make evident those who are truthful, and He will surely make evident the liars." [Al-ʿAnkabout: 2-3]

These tests are a separation between those who merely claim īmān and those who are truthful in it.

So be among the truthful. Remain steadfast.

If hardship is inevitable, then choose the hardship that brings you closer to Allah.
Choose the hardship that ends in Jannah.

Al-Ḥajjāj رحمه الله said:

“Indeed, being patient in refraining from what Allah has forbidden is easier than being patient with His punishment.”
[ʿUddat aṣ-Ṣābirīn wa Dhakhīrat ash-Shākirīn, p. 14]

Allah ﷻ says:

وَمَن يَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مَخْرَجًۭا ۝ وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ
“And whoever fears Allah — He will make for him a way out, and provide for him from where he does not expect.” [Al-Ṭalāq: 2-3]

Ask Allah for steadfastness, and He will grant it.

No servant sincerely asks Allah for firmness upon the truth, for beneficial knowledge, or for any form of khayr — except that Allah answers.

To believe otherwise is سوء ظن بالله. (having bad thoughts of Allah ﷻ)

So walk forward, upon the truth, even if your steps are small.

Hold firm, even if you stand alone.

And trust that every hardship you endure for Allah is never lost.

والله أعلم.

text.text: - أمُّ عبدِالرَّحمـٰن السُّودانيّة غفر الله لها

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text: Umm al-Faḍl — the wife of Shaykh al-Albānī رحمه الله — was asked:

text.text: “Being the wife of this virtuous scholar, have you found that his knowledge, his seeking of knowledge, and his teaching of people reduced his presence with you as the head of your household? And did this have any negative effect on his children?”

text:
she responded: "seeking knowledge never prevented the Shaykh from fulfilling any of his family duties. On the contrary, he—may Allah have mercy on him—was an example of a head of household who cooperated with his family.

Believe me, my son, he would often help me with the household chores to the point that I would feel shy because of it. He even once was mopping the balcony with me, and I said to him: “Shaykh, don’t embarrass us in front of the neighbors, lest they say this man does his wife’s work.” He replied: “This is not an embarrassment. Do you not know that the Prophet ﷺ used to serve his family?”

Whenever I asked him for anything related to the house—such as installing an extra shelf somewhere—he would study the matter and think about it. If he saw the request as reasonable, he would go ahead and do it himself. And if he needed to buy something for it, he would go in his car, purchase it, return, and carry out what I had pointed out to him.

He also had a fondness for outings—may Allah have mercy on him—the way of ahlul shām. The picnic basket (the outing supplies) was always in the car. We would go out together in spring and summer, and even in winter, to look at the snow and the winter scenery. He would humor me by drinking tea and coffee, even though he did not personally enjoy these two beverages. 

Yet he would never leave his books behind on any outing we took; the book was his companion wherever he went.

Often, I would wake up and not find him in bed. I would look for him and find him in the library, having turned on the lamp and become immersed in his books. I would be surprised, and he would say: “This is my beloved!!” — may Allah have mercy on him."

may Allah have mercy on them both.

text.text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

We would go out together in spring and summer, and even in winter, to look at the snow and the winter scenery. He would humor me by drinking tea and coffee, even though he did not personally enjoy these two beverages. 

Yet he would never leave his books behind on any outing we took; the book was his companion wherever he went.

Often, I would wake up and not find him in bed. I would look for him and find him in the library, having turned on the lamp and become immersed in his books. I would be surprised, and he would say: “This is my beloved!!” — may Allah have mercy on him."

may Allah have mercy on them both.
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
messages.text:
Abū Idrīs al-Khawlānī رحمه الله said:
Muʿādh ibn Jabal رضي الله عنه said:
“Ahead of you are trials in which wealth will become abundant, and the Qur’an will be widely circulated, until it is taken up by the believer and the hypocrite, the man and the woman, the young and the old, the slave and the free. Then a person will soon say: ‘What is wrong with the people that they do not follow me, when I have recited the Qur’an? They will not follow me until I introduce something new for them.’
So beware of newly invented matters, for whatever is invented is misguidance. And I warn you against the deviation (i.e slip) of the scholar, for indeed Shayṭān may utter a word of misguidance upon the tongue of a scholar, and a hypocrite may utter a word of truth.”
He said: I said to Muʿādh: “How will I know—may Allah have mercy on you—that a scholar has said a word of misguidance, and that a hypocrite has said a word of truth?”
He replied: "Yes. Avoid from the words of the scholar those statements that become widely known (for its falsehood) and about which it is said: ‘What is this?!’ But let that not turn you away from him, for perhaps he may retract. And accept the truth when you hear it, for indeed upon the truth there is light.”

Ṣaḥīḥ mawqūf. Reported by Abū Dāwūd (4611) and authenticated by al-Albānī in Ṣaḥīḥ Abī Dāwūd (3855).

Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
For the grief-stricken heart
For the heart that feels darkened with sorrow
For the heart that feels behind on blessings
For the heart that is lonely whilst he’s in a gathering of people
For the heart that is waiting for relief

This is the call to empty your heart of everything except the Most Merciful. Empty it of all worldly distractions and attach it to Allāh سبحانه وتعالى.

Allāh says in a ḥadīth qudsi:

text.text:
“O son of Ādam, busy yourself with My worship, and I will fill your heart with richness and contentment and fill your hands with provision; but if you do not do that, then I will fill your heart with poverty and busy your hands with distractions and I will not alleviate your poverty.”
[Sunan al-Tirmidhi‌ 2466]

text:
And do not let this worldly life distract you from what you are truly chasing.

Be with the companionship of the creation in body, and let your heart roam around your Beloved, let your soul reside in a higher abode.

This life was never meant to be a Jannah.

يقول أهل الجنة إذا دخلوها: {الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَذْهَبَ عَنَّا ٱلْحَزَن}

The people of Jannah when they enter it, they will say:
“All the praises and thanks be to Allāh Who has removed from us (all) grief and sorrow.”

text.text: [Sūrah Fāṭir:34]

text:
Await the reward of your Rabb. Indeed, Allāh does not fail in His promise.

{إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُخْلِفُ ٱلْمِيعَادَ}

text.text: [Sūrah Āl ʿImrān:9]
- أم مصعب الغانية الألمانية غفر الله لها

messages.author: أم مصعب

text.text: Are you in doubt about a certain matter..?

text: This religion is not based upon

doubts. We go upon what is certain.

As our Messenger ﷺ told us:
"دَعْ ما يُريبُك إلى ما لا يُريبُك"
'Leave that which makes you doubt for that which does not make you doubt.'
[

text.text: Sunan an-Nasa'i 5711
As an added benefit: hasten to do that which is best!

text:
The way we ask questions is wrong—“is this permissible..?”

Unfortunately, today the only thing that many of us are concerned with is permissibility. 

The Ṣaḥābah didn’t do certain things out of fear of falling into the ḥarām, even though they were totally permissible.

For example: it’s permissible to go to the store; you went and there was a male seller. He cracks a joke. You try to hold back your laugh, but you couldn’t. You end up laughing. Your voice rises. You just fell into the ḥarām.

This is not to say that you should not go to the store, however

text.text: not every permitted deed do we need to do

text:
.

Thus, we be careful when we embark upon actions that are permissible.

The Prophet ﷺ used to encourage us to do that which is

text.text: better,

text:
that which is more virtuous.

So we must differentiate between that which is permitted and that which is better. Just because
something is permissible does not mean it is encouraged.

We must, however, have a balanced understanding — we do not beat somebody down because they didn’t necessarily do what’s best; nor do we stick to what’s permitted and not race to do what’s best, and say “the main thing is that’s it’s permitted.”

No—this is not what the Companions رضي الله عنهم were upon, and they did not reach the level of acceptance with Allāh ﷻ  by following everything that was merely permitted. In other words,

text.text: they did not do the bare minimum

text:
, but in fact raced to do that which is higher and more praiseworthy.

And the believer follows in the footsteps of the salaf.

قالَ الله:فَٱسۡتَبِقُوا۟ ٱلۡخَیۡرَ ٰ⁠تِ
Meaning: “Rush and hasten to do good deeds.” [

text.text: Sūrah al-Baqarah:148

text:
]

والله أعلم

text.text:
Benefitted from Ustādha Umm Hyatt حفظها الله in Kitāb as-Ṣiyām dars
- أم مصعب الغانية الألمانية

text_entities.text: Are you in doubt about a certain matter..?
This religion is not based upon

doubts. We go upon what is certain.

As our Messenger ﷺ told us:
"دَعْ ما يُريبُك إلى ما لا يُريبُك"
'Leave that which makes you doubt for that which does not make you doubt.'
[
Sunan an-Nasa'i 5711
As an added benefit: hasten to do that which is best!

The way we ask questions is wrong—“is this permissible..?”

Unfortunately, today the only thing that many of us are concerned with is permissibility. 

The Ṣaḥābah didn’t do certain things out of fear of falling into the ḥarām, even though they were totally permissible.

For example: it’s permissible to go to the store; you went and there was a male seller. He cracks a joke. You try to hold back your laugh, but you couldn’t. You end up laughing. Your voice rises. You just fell into the ḥarām.

This is not to say that you should not go to the store, however
not every permitted deed do we need to do

.

Thus, we be careful when we embark upon actions that are permissible.

The Prophet ﷺ used to encourage us to do that which is
better,

that which is more virtuous.

So we must differentiate between that which is permitted and that which is better. Just because
something is permissible does not mean it is encouraged.

We must, however, have a balanced understanding — we do not beat somebody down because they didn’t necessarily do what’s best; nor do we stick to what’s permitted and not race to do what’s best, and say “the main thing is that’s it’s permitted.”

No—this is not what the Companions رضي الله عنهم were upon, and they did not reach the level of acceptance with Allāh ﷻ  by following everything that was merely permitted. In other words,
they did not do the bare minimum

, but in fact raced to do that which is higher and more praiseworthy.

And the believer follows in the footsteps of the salaf.

قالَ الله:فَٱسۡتَبِقُوا۟ ٱلۡخَیۡرَ ٰ⁠تِ
Meaning: “Rush and hasten to do good deeds.” [
Sūrah al-Baqarah:148

]

والله أعلم

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: Ibrāhīm at-Taymī رحمه الله said:

text:
"I imagined myself in Paradise, eating from its fruits, embracing its maidens, and enjoying its bliss. Then I said to myself: O soul, what do you wish for?
It replied: That I be returned to the world so I may increase in the deeds by which I attained this.
Then I imagined myself in the Fire, being burned by its flames, drinking from its boiling water, and eating from its Zaqqūm. I said to myself: What do you wish for?
It replied: That I be returned to the world so I may perform deeds by which I can be saved from this.
So I said to myself: O soul, you are already in a state where this wish is possible — so work!"

And it is reported that one of the Salaf used to dig a grave for himself. When he felt lazy in his deeds, he would descend into it, lie down in his niche, then say:

"O my soul, imagine that you have died and are now in your grave. What would you wish for?
It would say: That I be returned to the world so I may perform righteous deeds.
So he would say: You have already been granted your wish — so stand up and do righteous deeds!"

And know, may Allāh have mercy upon you, that the people of the graves wish that any one of them could Say a single tasbīḥ that would increase his good deeds, or be able to repent from some of his sins, or perform one rakʿah that would raise him in rank.

text.text: [Waṣiyyat Ibn Qudāmah al-Maqdisī رحمه الله p. 11]
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.text: While we are alive, we are living the very wish of the dead — the chance to act. Every moment is an opportunity that will soon become a regret if wasted. May Allāh make us among those who act before it is too late.

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
بسم الله الرحمـٰن الرحيم 

Many of us often look at what we’re missing, and it makes us feel lacking — ungrateful. We remind ourselves—and one another—not to focus on what we lack, but to look at what we have. Yet how many of us truly live by this advice? Very few.

Allah ﷻ says:

﴿وَقَلِيلٌ مِّنْ عِبَادِيَ الشَّكُورُ﴾
“And very few of My servants are truly grateful.” [Sabaʾ: 13] 

We allow ourselves to feel miserable over what we do not have, while forgetting the immense blessings Allah has already bestowed upon us. And this is the nature of mankind, as Allah ﷻ says:

﴿إِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ لِرَبِّهِ لَكَنُودٌ﴾
“Indeed, man is truly ungrateful to his Lord.” [al-ʿĀdiyāt: 6]

We forget even the most obvious of blessings — health, sight, sanity, emotions, literacy — and so much more endless blessings, each of them carrying more blessings within it. The greatest of them being that Allah ﷻ guided you to Islam.

Even more than that, He guided you to Ahlus Sunnah, to Ahlul Hadith, to the manhaj of the Salaf.

How can you allow yourself to overlook all this faḍl Allah bestowed upon you? How can sadness dominate your heart when you are surrounded by so many blessings—especially the greatest of them all?

Allah has not given anyone anything greater than Islam.

The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:

«عَلَيْكُمْ بِسُنَّتِي وَسُنَّةِ الْخُلَفَاءِ الرَّاشِدِينَ الْمَهْدِيِّينَ، عَضُّوا عَلَيْهَا بِالنَّوَاجِذِ»
“Hold firmly to my Sunnah and the Sunnah of the rightly guided caliphs, bite onto it with your molar teeth" [At-Tirmidhi 2676]

But how can we cling to something we do not value? How can we hold firmly to that which we belittle or fail to recognize as a blessing? How can one truly hold onto it when they are constantly fixated on worldly matters — while overlooking this faḍl that, had Allah withheld it, they would have been among the disbelievers? And that—being cursed by Allah and cast into the Hellfire—is a calamity greater than any worldly loss. There is nothing worse than that. 

Thank Allah for what He has granted you, and hold onto it tightly, just as the Prophet ﷺ commanded you.

Allah ﷻ says:

﴿وَكَانَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكَ عَظِيمًا﴾
"and ever has the favour of Allah upon you been great" [An-Nisāʾ:113]

Allah chose you—out of millions, billions, trillions of people from the beginning of time until its end—to guide you to the truth and make you among His servants.

What blessing could be greater than that?

Hold onto it firmly, because no one is guaranteed steadfastness until death. You are not promised that you will wake up tomorrow upon guidance.

The Prophet ﷺ warned us of trials so severe that a man may wake up a believer and go to sleep a disbeliever—or go to sleep a believer and wake up a disbeliever.

So ask Allah for steadfastness.

And thank Him—because if you are grateful, He will increase you:

﴿وَإِذْ تَأَذَّنَ رَبُّكُمْ لَئِن شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ ۖ وَلَئِن كَفَرْتُمْ إِنَّ عَذَابِي لَشَدِيدٌ﴾
"And [remember] when your Lord proclaimed, 'If you are grateful, I will surely increase you [in favor]; but if you deny, indeed, My punishment is severe.'" [Ibrāhīm: 7]

The only thing that is truly worth sadness is missing out on khayr you're capable of doing. 

والله المستعان.

text.text: -أم عبدالرحمـٰن السودانية غفر الله لها

text.text:
إِنَّ الكَرِيمَ إِذَا نَادَيْتَ قَالَ : نَعَمْ
‏فَكَيْفَ بِاللهِ ذِي الإِنْعَامِ وَالكَرَمِ؟
When you call upon a generous person, he says, “Yes.” So what about Allāh, the One full of blessings and generosity?

فَابْسُطْ لَهُ الكَفَّ لَنْ تَأْتِيكَ فَارِغَةً
‏فَقَدْ سَأَلْتَ الذِي سَوَّاكَ مِنْ عَدَمِ.
So raise your hands to Him — they will not return empty, for you are asking the One who created you from nothing.

messages.title: النقاب

text.text:
Questioner: Honorable Sheikh, the niqāb has trialed the men, the niqāb has trialed the men, the niqāb has trialed the men. Is there any advice for women about not tempting men? For many women have caused the deviation of many young men, and may Allāh reward you with good.

Sheikh ibn ʿUthaymīn رحمه الله answered:

text:
The niqāb is when a woman covers her face and leaves a small opening to see through, just enough for the eye alone, and without adorning the safir (the exposed part of the face around the eyes).

 Meaning she should not come with a beautifully decorated opening—no, it should be ordinary, and the opening should be narrow, just enough for her to see through, or just enough to see from it.

This was permissible in the time of the Messenger ﷺ, women used to wear the niqāb, evidence being that the Prophet ﷺ forbade the woman in the state of iḥrām from wearing the niqāb. This indicates that it was customary for women to wear it. 

However, in the present time, unfortunately some women have taken advantage of this by making the niqāb exceed what is necessary.

 You find a woman wearing a niqāb with a wide opening for her eyes, revealing her eyebrows, cheeks, and perhaps even her forehead, thus they have gone beyond with this.

For this reason, we refrained from issuing a fatwa permitting it — and we did not say it is forbidden; meaning we have not issued a fatwa prohibiting it — but we have refrained from issuing a fatwa permitting it to block the means and to prevent expansion [in its misuse]. 

The truth is, the issue of women is that many women have been deceived by what is published in newspapers and magazines and what is heard in the media, so a woman thinks she is like the pictures [in the magazines], with no concern except adornment and temptation.

 This is undoubtedly one of the calamities of this world.
____

text.text: tr: umm tamīm as-ṣūmāliyyah

text.text: The Fitnah of a Woman Uncovering Her Eyes in Front of Non-Mahram Men

text:
Shaykha Umm ʿAbdillāh Al-wādiʿiyyah حفظها الله said:

A poet said:

text.text:
She has a complexion like silk, and speech... Soft at the edges—neither nonsense nor scant.

And two eyes to which Allah said “Be,” and they were, They overpower the intelligent as wine does.

text: And another said:

text.text:
Indeed, the eyes whose edges are ḥūr-like.. Have killed us—then did not revive those they killed.

They strike down the intelligent until no movement remains, Though they are among Allah’s weakest of creations in strength.

text:
If people of intellect—those possessing sound reason—are tempted by a woman’s eyes, then how about others from the lowly and the weak-minded?!

Know that a woman bears sin if she becomes a cause of the temptation of men and the sickness of their hearts.

text.text:
It is authentically reported from Abū Saʿīd al-Khudrī رضي الله عنه that he said:

The Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out on the day of al-Aḍḥā or al-Fiṭr to the prayer place. He passed by the women and said: 
“O assembly of women, give charity, for I have been shown that you are the majority of the people of Hellfire.”
They said: “Why, O Messenger of Allah?”
He said: “You frequently curse and are ungrateful to your husbands. I have not seen anyone more deficient in intellect and religion who can take away the resolve of a firm, decisive man than one of you.”
They said: “What is the deficiency of our intellect and religion, O Messenger of Allah?”
He said: “Is not the testimony of a woman equal to half that of a man?”
They said: “Yes.”
He said: “That is from the deficiency of her intellect. Is it not that when she menstruates, she neither prays nor fasts?”
They said: “Yes.”
He said: “That is from the deficiency of her religion.”

Narrated by Abū ʿAbdillāh al-Bukhārī in his Ṣaḥīḥ (304).

text: Al-Ḥāfiẓ Ibn Ḥajar رحمه الله said in Fatḥ al-Bārī regarding this ḥadīth:

text.text: It appears to me that this is among the reasons they are the majority of the people of Hellfire;because if they become a cause for taking away the intellect of a firm and decisive man—until he does or says what is not appropriate—then they have shared with him in the sin, and have even added to it.

text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

It is authentically reported from Abū Saʿīd al-Khudrī رضي الله عنه that he said:

messages.text: The Ṣaḥābiyyāt رضي الله عنهن exposed nothing except what was absolutely necessary for sight. Today, Allāh has provided fabrics that allow vision without exposing the eyes at all—so what excuse remains? What, then, compels us to reveal them? Fear Allāh, my dear sisters, and do not open the door to fitnah.

text_entities.text: The Ṣaḥābiyyāt رضي الله عنهن exposed nothing except what was absolutely necessary for sight. Today, Allāh has provided fabrics that allow vision without exposing the eyes at all—so what excuse remains? What, then, compels us to reveal them? Fear Allāh, my dear sisters, and do not open the door to fitnah.

messages.text: The owners of this channel — drops of ʿilm (and the many channels associated with them) — very much hate the Salafī daʿwah from Yemen. They’ve spoke ill of the Yemeni Mashāyikh numerous times, saying Shaykh Yaḥyā has made his own sect and that and he’s not from Ahlus-Sunnah, likewise so are many of the scholars who defend him. Please stop promoting this channel. Just because a channel posts beneficial clips from other scholars doesn’t mean it’s fit to be shared. You have to do a full overview of their content before sharing their work. I see many channels falling into this mistake. Stop being so lazy when it comes to the concept of promoting channels on your own channel! Yes you get rewarded for spreading goodness, but through your failure to do proper research you open up more pathways for people to become confused and misguided. May Allāh protect us.

text.text: Do Not Apologize for Asking About Knowledge

text:
I was asked by someone about a point of knowledge who then said: I apologize for the disturbance.

So I told him:

text.text:
My son, do not apologize for asking, for asking about knowledge is not a disturbance, rather it is a path to guidance and elevation.

Whoever asks in order to learn has done well, and whoever is asked and answers is rewarded. 

Thus, do not feel embarrassed and reach out whenever you need to, and know that this excessive ḥayāʾ (shyness) may be from shayṭān to turn you from goodness.

- Written by Sheikh Faiṣal al-Ḥāshidī وفقه الله
Tr: Umm Tamīm aṣ-Ṣūmāliyyah

text:
بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم
Do you need motivation to pray Qiyām al-Layl?

What can be a better motivator than seeing how your Rabb describes the righteous — those He loves, those He praises in the Qurʾān, those He raises in rank?

Do you not want to be from the people about whom Allāh ﷻ says: 


  ﴿تَتَجَافَىٰ جُنُوبُهُمۡ عَنِ ٱلۡمَضَاجِعِ یَدۡعُونَ رَبَّهُمۡ خَوۡفࣰا وَطَمَعࣰا وَمِمَّا رَزَقۡنَـٰهُمۡ یُنفِقُونَ﴾ 

Their sides forsake their beds, to invoke their Lord in fear and hope, and they spend (in charity in Allâh’s Cause) out of what We have bestowed on them.
[As-Sajdah, Ayah 16]

﴿إِنَّ ٱلۡمُتَّقِینَ فِی جَنَّـٰتࣲ وَعُیُونٍ  ٠  ءَاخِذِینَ مَاۤ ءَاتَىٰهُمۡ رَبُّهُمۡۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُوا۟ قَبۡلَ ذَ ٰ⁠لِكَ مُحۡسِنِینَ ٠ كَانُوا۟ قَلِیلࣰا مِّنَ ٱلَّیۡلِ مَا یَهۡجَعُونَ ٠ وَبِٱلۡأَسۡحَارِ هُمۡ یَسۡتَغۡفِرُونَ﴾ 

Verily, the Muttaqûn (the pious) will be in the midst of Gardens and Springs (in the Paradise), Taking joy in the things which their Lord has given them. Verily, they were before this Muhsinûn (good-doers). They used to sleep but little by night [invoking their Lord and praying, with fear and hope]. And in the hours before dawn, they were (found) asking (Allāh) for forgiveness.
[Adh-Dhāriyāt, 15-18]

…or would you like to be from those who turn over in their warm beds while their Rabb descends to the lowest heaven saying: “

text.text: Who is calling upon Me that I may answer him? Who is asking from Me that I may give him? Who is seeking My forgiveness that I may forgive him?

text:
” [S‌ah‌i‌h‌ al-Bukha‌ri‌ 1145]

Your alarm rings.
Your bed feels comfortable.
Shayṭān whispers, “Just five more minutes.”

But the pious whisper, “

text.text: For Allāh

text:
.”

This life is short.
Comfort is temporary.
Jannah is forever.

Every night you are given a choice: 
the comfort of your bed — or the honour of standing before your Rabb, seeking closeness to Him ﷻ.

The question isn’t whether you can wake up.

It’s whether Allāh is worth more than sleep.

And He is always worth more.

Your Rabb is calling you while others are dreaming.

So which will you choose?

text.text:
عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ قَالَ جَاءَ جِبْرِيلُ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ يَا مُحَمَّدُ عِشْ مَا شِئْتَ فَإِنَّكَ مَيِّتٌ وَاعْمَلْ مَا شِئْتَ فَإِنَّكَ مَجْزِيٌّ بِهِ وَأَحْبِبْ مَنْ شِئْتَ فَإِنَّكَ مُفَارِقُهُ وَاعْلَمْ أَنَّ شَرَفَ الْمُؤْمِنِ قِيَامُ اللَّيْلِ وَعِزَّهُ اسْتِغْنَاؤُهُ عَنِ النَّاسِ

Sahl ibn Sa’d reported: Jibrīl came to the Prophet ﷺ and he said, “O Muhammad, live as you wish, for you will surely die. Work as you wish, for you will surely be rewarded according to it. Love whomever you wish, for you will surely depart from them. Know that the nobility of the believer is in prayer at night, and his honor is in his independence of people.”
[al-Muʻjam al-Awsat‌ lil-T‌abara‌ni‌ 4278; Hasan according to Al-Albānī]

text_entities.text:
بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم
Do you need motivation to pray Qiyām al-Layl?

What can be a better motivator than seeing how your Rabb describes the righteous — those He loves, those He praises in the Qurʾān, those He raises in rank?

Do you not want to be from the people about whom Allāh ﷻ says: 


  ﴿تَتَجَافَىٰ جُنُوبُهُمۡ عَنِ ٱلۡمَضَاجِعِ یَدۡعُونَ رَبَّهُمۡ خَوۡفࣰا وَطَمَعࣰا وَمِمَّا رَزَقۡنَـٰهُمۡ یُنفِقُونَ﴾ 

Their sides forsake their beds, to invoke their Lord in fear and hope, and they spend (in charity in Allâh’s Cause) out of what We have bestowed on them.
[As-Sajdah, Ayah 16]

﴿إِنَّ ٱلۡمُتَّقِینَ فِی جَنَّـٰتࣲ وَعُیُونٍ  ٠  ءَاخِذِینَ مَاۤ ءَاتَىٰهُمۡ رَبُّهُمۡۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُوا۟ قَبۡلَ ذَ ٰ⁠لِكَ مُحۡسِنِینَ ٠ كَانُوا۟ قَلِیلࣰا مِّنَ ٱلَّیۡلِ مَا یَهۡجَعُونَ ٠ وَبِٱلۡأَسۡحَارِ هُمۡ یَسۡتَغۡفِرُونَ﴾ 

Verily, the Muttaqûn (the pious) will be in the midst of Gardens and Springs (in the Paradise), Taking joy in the things which their Lord has given them. Verily, they were before this Muhsinûn (good-doers). They used to sleep but little by night [invoking their Lord and praying, with fear and hope]. And in the hours before dawn, they were (found) asking (Allāh) for forgiveness.
[Adh-Dhāriyāt, 15-18]

…or would you like to be from those who turn over in their warm beds while their Rabb descends to the lowest heaven saying: “
Who is calling upon Me that I may answer him? Who is asking from Me that I may give him? Who is seeking My forgiveness that I may forgive him?

” [S‌ah‌i‌h‌ al-Bukha‌ri‌ 1145]

Your alarm rings.
Your bed feels comfortable.
Shayṭān whispers, “Just five more minutes.”

But the pious whisper, “
For Allāh

.”

This life is short.
Comfort is temporary.
Jannah is forever.

Every night you are given a choice: 
the comfort of your bed — or the honour of standing before your Rabb, seeking closeness to Him ﷻ.

The question isn’t whether you can wake up.

It’s whether Allāh is worth more than sleep.

And He is always worth more.

Your Rabb is calling you while others are dreaming.

So which will you choose?

Sahl ibn Sa’d reported: Jibrīl came to the Prophet ﷺ and he said, “O Muhammad, live as you wish, for you will surely die. Work as you wish, for you will surely be rewarded according to it. Love whomever you wish, for you will surely depart from them. Know that the nobility of the believer is in prayer at night, and his honor is in his independence of people.”
[al-Muʻjam al-Awsat‌ lil-T‌abara‌ni‌ 4278; Hasan according to Al-Albānī]

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
It was narrated that Hudhaifah said:
"I prayed with the Prophet ﷺ one night. He started to recite Al-Baqarah and I thought, 'he will bow when he reaches one hundred,' but he carried on. I thought, 'he is going to recite the whole surah in one rak'ah,' but he carried on. He started to recite An-Nisā' and recited (the whole surah), then he started to recite Āli ʿImran and recited (the whole surah), reciting slowly. When he reached a verse that spoke of glorifying Allāh سبحانه وتعالى, he glorified Him. When he reached a verse that spoke of supplication, he made supplication. When he reached a verse that spoke of seeking refuge with Allāh, he sought refuge with Him. Then he bowed and said: 'Subhana Rabbiyal-Azim.(Glory be to my Lord Almighty)', and he bowed for almost as long as he had stood. Then he raised his head and said: 'Sami Allāhu liman hamidah (Allāh hears those who praise Him)', and he stood for almost as long as he had bowed. Then he prostrated and started to say: Subhana Rabbiyal-'Ala (Glory be to my Lord Most High),' and he prostrated for almost as long as he had bowed.'"
[Sunan an-Nasā'i 1664]

text.text: Brief clarification of this ḥadīth

Our Shaykha, Oum ʿAbdillah Al-Briṭāniyyah حفظها الله shared with us stories of Ramaḍān in Dammāj. So many people travelled to Dammāj to simply experience the tarāwīḥ prayers there, as Dammāj had become famous for its tarāwīḥ prayers. 

Shaykh Yaḥyā and Sheikh Aḥmad Waṣābī would lead extremely long tarāwīḥ prayers; especially in the last ten nights—they would be awake almost the entire night.

In the last ten nights, they would complete the Qurʾān more than once. The prayers would begin around 9:00 p.m. and end just fifteen minutes before the Fajr adhān.

They would start straight after ‘Ishā’, and the shuyūkh would recite long portions of Qurʾān—continuously.

The first two rak‘ahs alone could last an hour and a half. The rukū‘ and sujūd were extremely long — you really had to be physically fit. Each sujūd could last at least ten minutes, without exaggeration — to the point where they would stretch their arms out and then bring them back in again.

text:
The exertion is what makes it beautiful.

You may have thought, “these people only exist in the past.” However, you don’t have to be confined to the generation of the people of today. Elevate yourself and connect with the people of the past. Read the biographies of the salaf and live with them vicariously.

The Prophet ﷺ told us “

text.text: it is the habit of the righteous who came before you.

text:
”
[Sunan al-Tirmidhi‌ 3549]

So join their ranks. Compare yourself to those who are better.

In every generation we have them — people who are not from this era, people who strive in worship, people who do not busy themselves with distractions but are focused on preparing to meet their Lord.

The Prophet ﷺ would pray and pray till his feet would swell. 

This is the Sunnah.

Make this your goal. 

You only have one life.

Don’t measure yourself by the standard of the lazy when the standard of the righteous is before you.

Strive, even when it is hard.
Pray, even if you are tired.
Remember this life is simply an investment for the next. How much do you wish to invest into it? How badly do you want it?

And in the end, it will be worth it.

إن شاء الله تعالى

text.text: - Umm Muṣʿab al-Ghāniyyah al-Almāniyyah غفر الله لها

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text:
Al-Imām Ibn al-Qayyim رحمه الله says:

"Complaining to Him—Glorified is He—does not contradict beautiful patience. Rather, the servant turning away from complaining to anyone else entirely, and directing his complaint solely to Him,

text.text: is patience.

text:
Allāh—Glorified is He—tests His servant to hear his complaint, his pleading, and his supplication. He has condemned those who do not humble themselves before Him and do not submit to Him at the time of trial, as He said:

﴾And indeed We seized them with punishment, but they did not humble themselves to their Lord, nor did they invoke (Allāh) with submission to Him.﴿ 
[Surat Al-Mu'minūn: 76]

The servant is too weak to pretend to be patient and self-sufficient before his Lord. The Lord—Exalted is He—does not want His servant to pretend patience before Him. Rather, He wants him to submit and plead to Him. He—Exalted is He—detests the one who complains about Him to His creation, and He loves the one who complains of his condition to Him."

'Uddat aṣ-Ṣābirīn wa dhakhīrat ash-Shākirīn (Page 63)'

text.text: Tr: Umm Tamīm aṣ-Ṣūmāliyyah

text.text: Reciting the Qurʾān Hastily

text: It was narrated In “Ṣaḥīḥ al-Bukhārī”¹ from Qatādah, he said: Anas was asked: How was the recitation of the Prophet ﷺ? He said: «He would prolong the sounds in his recitation, then he recited “In the name of Allāh, the most Gracious, the most Merciful,” prolonging ‘Bismillāh’, ‘ar-Raḥmān’, and ‘ar-Raḥīm’.»

text.text: Explanation:

text:
The ḥadīth indicates that the Prophet's ﷺ recitation was characterized by elongation (madd).

Ibn al-Mulaqqin said: "His Lord—Glorified and Exalted be He—commanded him to recite with careful recitation [tartīl] and to recite it deliberately, and not to move his tongue hastily with it, so he complied to his Lord's command and would recite it at a measured pace, to show his Ummah how they should recite and how they should reflect upon the Qurʾān and understand it."²

That was how the recitation of the Prophet ﷺ was—both within the prayer and outside of it—and it must be taken as a model to follow.

Ibn ʿAbbās –may Allāh be pleased with them– said: «To recite Sūrat al-Baqarah and āl-ʿImrān with a measured pace and reflection is more beloved to me than reciting the entire Qur’ān hastily."³

1) Narrated by Buhkārī (5046).
2) At-Tawḍīḥ in explaining al-Jāmi‘ aṣ-Ṣaḥīh (24/154).
3) Reported by al-Bayhaqī in Al-Kubrā (4490).

text.text:
Book: From the Mistakes of those who Fast by Sheikh Faiṣal al-Ḥāshidī
Tr: Umm Tamīm aṣ-Ṣūmāliyyah

Explanation:

The ḥadīth indicates that the Prophet's ﷺ recitation was characterized by elongation (madd).

Ibn al-Mulaqqin said: "His Lord—Glorified and Exalted be He—commanded him to recite with careful recitation [tartīl] and to recite it deliberately, and not to move his tongue hastily with it, so he complied to his Lord's command and would recite it at a measured pace, to show his Ummah how they should recite and how they should reflect upon the Qurʾān and understand it."²

That was how the recitation of the Prophet ﷺ was—both within the prayer and outside of it—and it must be taken as a model to follow.

Ibn ʿAbbās –may Allāh be pleased with them– said: «To recite Sūrat al-Baqarah and āl-ʿImrān with a measured pace and reflection is more beloved to me than reciting the entire Qur’ān hastily."³

1) Narrated by Buhkārī (5046).
2) At-Tawḍīḥ in explaining al-Jāmi‘ aṣ-Ṣaḥīh (24/154).

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text: Abū Dharr al-Ghifārī رضي الله عنه narrated that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:

text.text: “Indeed, I see what you do not see, and I hear what you do not hear. The sky has creaked, (أطَّت) and it has every right to creak.

text: [meaning it produced a sound and groaned; aṭīṭ refers to the sound made by camels, or the sound of saddles]

text.text: There is no space the width of four fingers in it except that an angel has placed his forehead there in prostration to Allah.

text: [meaning that the vast number of angels in the sky has weighed upon it until it creaked.]

text.text: By Allah, if you knew what I know,

text: [if you were to know what I know of the enormity, terror, and severity of the matter]

text.text: you would laugh little and weep much; you would not take pleasure with women upon beds,

text: [meaning you would find no ease or enjoyment in intimacy with your wives because of fear, panic, and the severity and horror of the matter.]

text.text: and you would go out to the open places

text: [you would leave your homes and go out into roads and pathways]

text.text: crying out to Allah.

text:
[you would supplicate humbly to Allah, begging Him to save you, forgive you, and pardon you.]”
Abū Dharr said:

text.text: “I wish that I were a tree that is cut down.”

text:
[Meaning: I would have wished to be a tree that is cut and uprooted from its place, so that it perishes and comes to an end.]

graded: ḥasan gharīb

text.text: — Sunan at-Tirmidhi (2312)
dorar.net

text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

text_entities.text: Abū Dharr al-Ghifārī رضي الله عنه narrated that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
“Indeed, I see what you do not see, and I hear what you do not hear. The sky has creaked, (أطَّت) and it has every right to creak.
[meaning it produced a sound and groaned; aṭīṭ refers to the sound made by camels, or the sound of saddles]
There is no space the width of four fingers in it except that an angel has placed his forehead there in prostration to Allah.
[meaning that the vast number of angels in the sky has weighed upon it until it creaked.]
By Allah, if you knew what I know,
[if you were to know what I know of the enormity, terror, and severity of the matter]
you would laugh little and weep much; you would not take pleasure with women upon beds,
[meaning you would find no ease or enjoyment in intimacy with your wives because of fear, panic, and the severity and horror of the matter.]
and you would go out to the open places
[you would leave your homes and go out into roads and pathways]
crying out to Allah.

[you would supplicate humbly to Allah, begging Him to save you, forgive you, and pardon you.]”
Abū Dharr said:
“I wish that I were a tree that is cut down.”

[Meaning: I would have wished to be a tree that is cut and uprooted from its place, so that it perishes and comes to an end.]

graded: ḥasan gharīb
— Sunan at-Tirmidhi (2312)
dorar.net
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.title: الشيخ الألباني ما معنى قول الترمذي " حسن غريب "

text.text:
Benefit

What does ḥasan gharīb mean?

text:
Shaykh al-Albānī رحمه الله said:

Imām —or al-Ḥāfiẓ— at-Tirmidhī رحمه الله indicates that every chain of narration for which he says “ḥasan gharīb” means that one of its narrators has some slight deficiency in precision and retention of the ḥadīth. 

Thus, the ḥadīth is ḥasan in itself; it is not ṣaḥīḥ—because a ṣaḥīḥ ḥadīth requires the narrator to be trustworthy, precise, and strong in memorization—as for a ḥasan ḥadīth, the narrator’s precision and memorization are lesser than that of a ṣaḥīḥ narrator.

So when he says “ḥasan gharīb,” he means that the chain is ḥasan in itself, and that one of its narrators has a slight weakness in memorization.

This is what is intended by at-Tirmidhī’s terminology.

[translation from 4:00-5:06]

text.text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

Imām —or al-Ḥāfiẓ— at-Tirmidhī رحمه الله indicates that every chain of narration for which he says “ḥasan gharīb” means that one of its narrators has some slight deficiency in precision and retention of the ḥadīth.

text.text: The Virtues of Sending Salutations on the Prophet ﷺ

Ubayy ibn Kaʿb reported: I said, “O Messenger of Allah, I frequently send salutations upon you. How much of my prayers (duʿāʾ) should I assign for making supplications for you?” The Prophet ﷺ said, “As you wish (whatever portion you desire).” I said, “A fourth?” The Prophet ﷺ said, “As you wish, (whatever portion you desire Ubayy) but if you were to add to this, this is better for you.” I said, “A half?” The Prophet ﷺ said, “As you wish, (whatever portion you desire Ubayy) but if you were to add to this, this is better for you.” I said, “Two-thirds?” The Prophet ﷺ said, “As you wish, (whatever portion you desire Ubayy) but if you were to add to this, this is better for you.” I said, “Should I say all of my prayers for blessings upon you?” The Prophet ﷺ said, “If so, your worries will be resolved, and your sins will be forgiven.”

[Sunan al-Tirmidhī 2457]

text:
So the entire time—in the times that you make duʿāʾ—instead of making duʿāʾ for yourself, or your family members or anything of that nature, you are turning that entire time into time in which you are sending ṣalāt upon the Messenger ﷺ.

If this is the case, you will be forgiven of your sins and whatever you have of worries will be sufficed.

text.text: Subḥanallāh

text: , only by sending salutations upon the Prophet ﷺ — the one sent as an Imām and a leader for us, the one sent to remove us from darkness into the light — repeating this in the time of your duʿāʾ, and giving your entire time of duʿāʾ for yourself to him ﷺ you will be sufficed of your worries.

text.text: How many worries do we have?

text:
We know within the depths of ourselves there are things that bother us: family troubles, financial issues, personal struggles, illnesses. 

By designating your entire time of supplication to the Prophet ﷺ, then you find Allāh ﷻ suffices you of your worries. Not only that, but you are forgiven of your sins!

And how in need are we to be forgiven of our sins, not just in this period (the blessed month of Ramaḍān), but throughout the year.

text.text:
Sheikh Abū ʿAtiyyah الله يحفظه; taken from a post-ʿishāʾ reminder
- Umm Muṣʿab al-Ghāniyyah al-Almāniyyah

text.text: From the supplications of the Prophet ﷺ is that he used to say:
“And I ask You for the coolness (comfort) of life after death.”

text: Authenticated by al-Albānī in Takhrīj Kitāb as-Sunnah (no. 427) and by Imām al-Wādiʿī in Aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥ al-Musnad رحمة الله عليهما.

text.text: Ibn al-Qayyim رحمه الله said in Ighāthat al-Lahfān (1/29):

“Since life in this worldly abode is never truly cool (comfortable) for anyone whatsoever; rather, it is filled with choking griefs and distress, and surrounded by inward and outward pains — he asked for the coolness of life after death"

text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

text:
‘A’ishah (رضي الله عنها) said:

كانَ النبيُّ ﷺ إذَا دَخَلَ العَشْر شَدَّ مِئْزَرَهُ، وأَحْيَا لَيْلَهُ، وأَيْقَظَ أهْلَهُ.

“When the last ten (nights of Ramadan) began, the Prophet ﷺ would tighten his lower garment, spend the night in worship, and awaken his family.”

Sahih al-Bukhari (2024), Sahih Muslim (1174)

text.text:
متى يكون النقص كمالاً؟!

بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم
الحمد لله، والصلاة والسلام على نبيه ومصطفاه

قال الحافظ ابن رجب رحمه الله :
كلُّ شيءٍ ناقصٌ في عُرفِ الناس في الدّنيا
حتى إذا انتَسَبَ إلى طاعته ورضاه
فهو الكاملُ في الحقيقة

خلوف أفواه الصائمين له = أطيب من ريح المسك
عري المحرمين لزيارة بيته = أجمل مِن لباس الحلل
انكسار المخبتين لعظمته = هو الجبر
ذلُّ الخائفين مِن سطوته = هو العزّ
بذلُ النفوس للقتل في سبيله = هو الحياة
جوع الصائمين لأجله = هو الشبع
عطشهم في طلب مرضاته = هو الرّيّ
نَصَب المجتهدين في خدمته = هو الراحة
ذلُّ الفتى في الحب مكرمة ... وخضوعه لحبيبه شرف
هبّت اليوم على القلوب نفحةٌ مِن نفحات نسيم القرب
سعى سمسارُ المواعظ للمهجورين في الصلح
وصلت البشارةُ للمنقطعين بالوصل، وللمذنبين بالعفو، والمستوجبين النار بالعتق
لَمّا سُلسل الشيطان في شهر رمضان، وخمدت نيران الشهوات بالصيام؛ انعزل سلطانُ الهوى
وصارت الدولةُ لحاكم العقل بالعدل
فلم يبق للعاصي عذر
يا غيوم الغفلة عن القلوب! تقشعي
يا شموس التقوى والإيمان! اطلعي
يا صحائف أعمال الصائمين! ارتفعي
يا قلوب الصائمين! اخشعي
يا أقدام المتهجدين! اسجدي لربك واركعي
يا عيون المجتهدين! لا تهجعي
يا ذنوب التائبين! لا ترجعي
يا أرض الهوى! ابلعي ماءك، ويا سماء النفوس! أقلعي
يا همم المحبين! بغير الله لا تقنعي
قد مدت في هذه الأيام موائد الإنعام للصوام فما منكم إلا مَن دُعي:
{يَا قَوْمَنَا أَجِيبُوا دَاعِيَ اللَّهِ} [الاحقاف: 31]
ويا همم المؤمنين! اسرعي
فطوبى لمن أجاب
فأصاب
وويل لمن طرد عن الباب
وما دعي.

"بغية الإنسان من وظائف رمضان" ، ص 21 و22 ، باختصار.

سُكَينة بنت محمد ناصر الدين الألبانية في 8/18/2009

text.text: When Does Deficiency Become Perfection?

text:
بسم الله الرحمـٰن الرحيم 

All praise is due to Allāh, and prayers and peace be upon His Prophet and Chosen One.

Al-Ḥāfiẓ Ibn Rajab — may Allāh have mercy on him — said:

text.text: Everything that people consider deficient in this worldly life—when it becomes connected to obedience to Allāh and His pleasure—is, in reality, true perfection.

text:
• The odor from the mouths of those who fast for His sake is more pleasant to Him than the fragrance of musk.
• The bareness of those in iḥrām when visiting His House is more beautiful than fine garments.
• The brokenness of the humble before His greatness is true restoration.
• The humility of those who fear His might is true honour.
• The striving of souls to be killed in His path is true life.
• The hunger of those who fast for His sake is true fullness.
• Their thirst in seeking His pleasure is true quenching.
• The fatigue of the diligent in serving Him is true rest.

 The humiliation of a young man in love is a virtue, and his submission to his Beloved is an honour.

Today a breeze from the breezes of nearness has blown upon the hearts. The broker of admonitions has come seeking reconciliation with those who were abandoned.

Glad tidings have reached those who were cut off—that they will be reconnected, those who sinned—that they will be forgiven, and those deserving the Fire—that they will be freed.

When the devils were chained in the month of Ramaḍān, and the fires of desires were extinguished through fasting. The authority of whims withdrew, and the rule became for the ruler of intellect with justice, so no excuse remained for the sinner.

O clouds of heedlessness from the hearts—disperse!
O suns of piety and faith—rise!
O records of the deeds of those fasting—ascend!
O hearts of those fasting—be humbled in khushūʿ!
O feet of those who pray at night—prostrate to your Lord and bow!
O eyes of the striving ones—do not sleep!
O sins of the repentant—do not return!
O earth of desires—swallow your water; and O sky of the souls—cease!
O aspirations of the lovers—be not content with anything besides Allāh!

In these days the tables of divine favour have been spread for those who fast—so none of you except that he has been invited:

text.text: «O our people! Respond (with obedience) to Allāh’s Caller..» [al-Aḥqāf: 31]

text:
And O ambitions of the believers—hasten!
Glad tidings to the one who responded, and thus attained.
And woe to the one who was turned away from the door and had not been invited.

___
“Bughyat al-Insān min Waẓāʾif Ramaḍān” p. 21-22, Summarized.

Written by: Shaykha Sukaynah bint Muḥammad Nāṣiruddeen al-Albāniyyah.

text.text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

text.text: Knowing When Laylat al-Qadr Occurs

text:
Questioner: How does a Muslim know that he has encountered Laylat al-Qadr while dilgently seeking it during the nights that are narrated from the Prophet ﷺ?

Al-ʾImām al-ʾAlbānī -may Allāh have mercy on him-: That is a sentimental matter felt by everyone whom Allāh—Blessed and Exalted is He—has favored to witness Laylat al-Qadr. This is because on this night the person turns to worshiping Allāh—Mighty and Majestic is He—to His remembrance, and to prayer. Allāh—Mighty and Majestic—then reveals to some of His servants a feeling that not even the righteous experience at other times, this feeling is what can be relied upon.

ʿĀʾishah -may Allāh be pleased with her- conveyed to the Messenger ﷺ a question that indicates whether a person can sense witnessing Laylat al-Qadr.

text.text: She asked the Prophet ﷺ: "O Messenger of Allāh, if I know which night is Laylat al-Qadr, what should I say during it?" He said: «Say: O Allāh, You are Pardoning and love pardon, so pardon me.»

text:
In this ḥadīth, there are two benefits:

1- A Muslim can have a personal, subjective feeling of encountering Laylat al-Qadr. 
2- If he feels that, the best supplication to make is this one: "O Allāh, You are Pardoning and love pardon, so pardon me."

On this occasion, it is mentioned this book of ours, "At-Targhīb," in some lessons, that the best thing a person can ask his Lord—Blessed and Exalted is He—is for pardon and well-being (ʿāfiyah) in this world and the Hereafter.

Yes, there are some signs and physical indicators of Laylat al-Qadr, but not everyone who witnesses and knows Laylat al-Qadr can see these. That is because some of these signs relate to the general external atmosphere. For example, the night might be neither hot nor cold, but rather temperate. However, a person might be in an environment where he cannot perceive the natural climate of the area.

There is also a sign, but it appears after Laylat al-Qadr in the morning of that night at sunrise. The Prophet ﷺ informed us that the sun rises on the morning of Laylat al-Qadr appearing like a moon, without rays. That is how the sun rises on the morning of Laylat al-Qadr, and this has been observed by some righteous people who cared about seeing and noticing it on many nights of Laylat al-Qadr.

What's important regarding the devoted worshipper is not to cling to such physical phenomena, because these phenomena are general, meaning they pertain to the nature of the climate. Not everyone who lives in that climate shares in witnessing Laylat al-Qadr, meaning: in having that spiritual clarity at one of the moments during that blessed night, such that Allāh—Mighty and Majestic—bestows upon them His mercy and grace, inspiring one and causing him to supplicate with what has preceded along with other supplications.

Verily physical signs are signs, but they do not indicate that everyone who witnesses or experiences them has attained Laylat al-Qadr—and this is the reality. However, the aspect that should be focused on is the spiritual clarity within oneself and the feeling of witnessing Laylat al-Qadr, and turning to Allāh with prescribed supplications.

This is the aspect we should focus on and be concerned with, hoping that Allāh—Mighty and Majestic—may bestow it upon us by His grace.

Tr: Umm Tamīm aṣ-Ṣūmāliyyah

text_entities.text: Knowing When Laylat al-Qadr Occurs

Questioner: How does a Muslim know that he has encountered Laylat al-Qadr while dilgently seeking it during the nights that are narrated from the Prophet ﷺ?

Al-ʾImām al-ʾAlbānī -may Allāh have mercy on him-: That is a sentimental matter felt by everyone whom Allāh—Blessed and Exalted is He—has favored to witness Laylat al-Qadr. This is because on this night the person turns to worshiping Allāh—Mighty and Majestic is He—to His remembrance, and to prayer. Allāh—Mighty and Majestic—then reveals to some of His servants a feeling that not even the righteous experience at other times, this feeling is what can be relied upon.

ʿĀʾishah -may Allāh be pleased with her- conveyed to the Messenger ﷺ a question that indicates whether a person can sense witnessing Laylat al-Qadr.
She asked the Prophet ﷺ: "O Messenger of Allāh, if I know which night is Laylat al-Qadr, what should I say during it?" He said: «Say: O Allāh, You are Pardoning and love pardon, so pardon me.»

In this ḥadīth, there are two benefits:

1- A Muslim can have a personal, subjective feeling of encountering Laylat al-Qadr. 
2- If he feels that, the best supplication to make is this one: "O Allāh, You are Pardoning and love pardon, so pardon me."

On this occasion, it is mentioned this book of ours, "At-Targhīb," in some lessons, that the best thing a person can ask his Lord—Blessed and Exalted is He—is for pardon and well-being (ʿāfiyah) in this world and the Hereafter.

Yes, there are some signs and physical indicators of Laylat al-Qadr, but not everyone who witnesses and knows Laylat al-Qadr can see these. That is because some of these signs relate to the general external atmosphere. For example, the night might be neither hot nor cold, but rather temperate. However, a person might be in an environment where he cannot perceive the natural climate of the area.

There is also a sign, but it appears after Laylat al-Qadr in the morning of that night at sunrise. The Prophet ﷺ informed us that the sun rises on the morning of Laylat al-Qadr appearing like a moon, without rays. That is how the sun rises on the morning of Laylat al-Qadr, and this has been observed by some righteous people who cared about seeing and noticing it on many nights of Laylat al-Qadr.

What's important regarding the devoted worshipper is not to cling to such physical phenomena, because these phenomena are general, meaning they pertain to the nature of the climate. Not everyone who lives in that climate shares in witnessing Laylat al-Qadr, meaning: in having that spiritual clarity at one of the moments during that blessed night, such that Allāh—Mighty and Majestic—bestows upon them His mercy and grace, inspiring one and causing him to supplicate with what has preceded along with other supplications.

Verily physical signs are signs, but they do not indicate that everyone who witnesses or experiences them has attained Laylat al-Qadr—and this is the reality. However, the aspect that should be focused on is the spiritual clarity within oneself and the feeling of witnessing Laylat al-Qadr, and turning to Allāh with prescribed supplications.

This is the aspect we should focus on and be concerned with, hoping that Allāh—Mighty and Majestic—may bestow it upon us by His grace.

Tr: Umm Tamīm aṣ-Ṣūmāliyyah

text.text: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:

إنَّ الرِّفْقَ لا يَكونُ في شَيءٍ إلَّا زانَهُ، ولا يُنْزَعُ مِن شَيءٍ إلَّا شانَهُ.
“Gentleness is not present in anything except that it beautifies it, and it is not removed from anything except that it disgraces it.”

text: Ṣaḥīḥ Muslim (2594)

text.text:
"ذَلِكَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَنْ يَشَاءُ وَاللَّهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ"

'That is the Grace of Allâh, which He bestows on whom He wills. And Allâh is the Owner of Mighty Grace.' [al-Jumuʿah: 4]
(hilāli & khān translation)
Tarbiyah-related benefits:

text: In Allāh’s statement:

text.text: 'That is the Grace of Allâh, which He bestows on whom He wills. And Allâh is the Owner of Mighty Grace.'

text: we understand that whoever receives a portion of the religion of Islām has indeed received a great favour. The blessing of Allāh has become immense upon them. How urgently they need, then, to be grateful for this blessing, to ask Allāh for its continuance, and to remain steadfast upon it until death. Dying upon it completes and perfects the blessing!

text.text: Reference: Laṭā’if al-Ma‘ārif by Ibn Rajab (p. 85).

text:
–
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: Allāh is full of bounty.

text:
How could one ever truly comprehend the immense gift of Islām?

If Allāh has given you Islām, then know that He has given you something greater than this world and everything in it.

It is a gift greater than wealth, greater than health, greater than every worldly possession that people spend their lives chasing.

Allāh chose

text.text: you

text:
— specifically you — to carry this light and be among those who worship Him ﷻ.

This is the greatest blessing.

Cherish it. Preserve it. Thank Allāh for it.

For how many were given this blessing yet took it for granted?
How many were given this light, yet darkened it with their deeds?
How many were upon the straight path, then later strayed from it?

So how can a believer not fear losing such a niʿmah?
How can he not guard it with everything he has?

For this blessing is too precious to lose.

text.text: ﴿ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱصۡطَفَىٰ لَكُمُ ٱلدِّینَ فَلَا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنتُم مُّسۡلِمُونَ ﴾
Allāh has chosen for you the (true) religion, then die not except in the Faith of Islām (as Muslims).”

text:
— [Al-Baqarah: 132]

يا مقلب القلوب ثبت قلوبنا على دينك

-أم مصعب الغانية الألمانية

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚

text.text: Informing the People of ʾĪmān of the Detailed Ruling Regarding Sadness Over the Departure of Ramaḍān 🌙

text:
The time of departure has drawn near, and just as we used to see messages of joy and longing, we now see messages of sadness and departure. 

People have differed regarding the ruling on feeling sad over its departure. Some said it is not allowed, others said feeling sadness at its departure is like joy at its arrival.

The detailed answer is as follows:

Firstly: The original ruling is to show joy, due to the statement of the Prophet ﷺ

text.text: «There are two joys for the fasting person: the joy when he breaks his fast, and the joy of when he meets his Lord.»

text:
Joy at breaking the fast is of two types.

1️⃣ Joy at the time of ifṭār at the end of the daily fast, and this is a recurring joy throughout the month.
2️⃣ Joy at the time of ʿĪd al-Fiṭr at the end of the monthly fast, and this is the final joy attached to breaking the fast. This is what is intended by the ḥadīth, and its meaning also includes the first [type].

Know that people, in their joy, are two types:
🔴 The first type:
A joy that entails hatred of fasting and happiness at being relieved from it. This is nonexistent except with hypocrites.
🟢 The second type:
A joy that does not mean relief from something hated, but rather a transition from one joy to another.

Accordingly, there are two joys:
● The joy of worship.
● The joy of completing worship.
All of this is permissible and legislated without doubt, and it is what is intended in the ḥadīth.

What remains is the summary of this situation:

text.text: Is it correct to feel sadness over the departure of Ramaḍān?

text:
The answer is from two angles.

The First Angle:
The original ruling is to show joy, based on two evidences:

1️⃣ The statement of Allāh:

text.text: "(He wants that you) must complete the same number (of days), and that you must magnify Allāh [i.e. to say Takbīr] for having guided you so that you may be grateful to Him."

text:
This indicates joy from two aspects:
🌴 First: Takbīr
The takbīr at the end of fasting contradicts the display of sadness; rather, it is a symbol of joy, celebration, and gladness.
🌴 Second: Gratitude
Gratitude is not for a calamity, but for a blessing — and a blessing necessitates joy, not sadness.

2️⃣ Mentioned in the ḥadīth:

text.text: «There are two joys for the fasting person: the joy when he breaks his fast, and the joy of when he meets his Lord.»

text:
Just as you do not feel sadness at meeting your Lord, likewise you should not feel sadness at breaking your fast as the two joys are paired together.

The Second Angle:
Sadness is an incidental matter that opposes the foundation, and it has two cases.

🚫 First case: That a person feels sad wishing Ramaḍān were longer, believing that this would be better. This sadness is invalid and a misguidance, because it opposes what Allāh has legislated, implying that something else is better than what Allāh decreed, and we seek refuge in Allāh.

✅️ Second case: That a person feels sad due to missing out on good because of his own shortcomings, negligence, or preoccupation. This is permissible, even if it comes late, as it is a form of regret over missing righteous deeds.

However, its permissibility is conditional upon not publicizing or displaying this sadness, because the Sharīʿah commands takbīr and joy.

text.text: Thus, this is a private state that remains with the individual and should not extend to others.

text:
*It was not from the way of the Salaf to display sadness during the days of ʿĪd at all; rather this contradicts their guidance.*

Written by:

text.text: Abū al-ʿAbbās Anwar ibn Maḥmūd ar-Rifāʿī

text:
1441 H
Tr: Umm Tamīm aṣ-Ṣūmāliyyah

The time of departure has drawn near, and just as we used to see messages of joy and longing, we now see messages of sadness and departure.

The detailed answer is as follows:

Firstly: The original ruling is to show joy, due to the statement of the Prophet ﷺ
«There are two joys for the fasting person: the joy when he breaks his fast, and the joy of when he meets his Lord.»

Joy at breaking the fast is of two types.

1️⃣ Joy at the time of ifṭār at the end of the daily fast, and this is a recurring joy throughout the month.
2️⃣ Joy at the time of ʿĪd al-Fiṭr at the end of the monthly fast, and this is the final joy attached to breaking the fast. This is what is intended by the ḥadīth, and its meaning also includes the first [type].

🟢 The second type:
A joy that does not mean relief from something hated, but rather a transition from one joy to another.

Accordingly, there are two joys:
● The joy of worship.
● The joy of completing worship.
All of this is permissible and legislated without doubt, and it is what is intended in the ḥadīth.

What remains is the summary of this situation:
Is it correct to feel sadness over the departure of Ramaḍān?

The answer is from two angles.

The First Angle:
The original ruling is to show joy, based on two evidences:

1️⃣ The statement of Allāh:
"(He wants that you) must complete the same number (of days), and that you must magnify Allāh [i.e. to say Takbīr] for having guided you so that you may be grateful to Him."

This indicates joy from two aspects:
🌴 First: Takbīr
The takbīr at the end of fasting contradicts the display of sadness; rather, it is a symbol of joy, celebration, and gladness.
🌴 Second: Gratitude
Gratitude is not for a calamity, but for a blessing — and a blessing necessitates joy, not sadness.

2️⃣ Mentioned in the ḥadīth:
«There are two joys for the fasting person: the joy when he breaks his fast, and the joy of when he meets his Lord.»

Just as you do not feel sadness at meeting your Lord, likewise you should not feel sadness at breaking your fast as the two joys are paired together.

The Second Angle:
Sadness is an incidental matter that opposes the foundation, and it has two cases.

🚫 First case: That a person feels sad wishing Ramaḍān were longer, believing that this would be better. This sadness is invalid and a misguidance, because it opposes what Allāh has legislated, implying that something else is better than what Allāh decreed, and we seek refuge in Allāh.

✅️ Second case: That a person feels sad due to missing out on good because of his own shortcomings, negligence, or preoccupation. This is permissible, even if it comes late, as it is a form of regret over missing righteous deeds.

However, its permissibility is conditional upon not publicizing or displaying this sadness, because the Sharīʿah commands takbīr and joy.

*It was not from the way of the Salaf to display sadness during the days of ʿĪd at all; rather this contradicts their guidance.*

Written by:
Abū al-ʿAbbās Anwar ibn Maḥmūd ar-Rifāʿī

1441 H

text.text: Eid Prayer for Women

text:
Shaykh ibn ʿUthaymīn رحمه الله said:

"Even women are encouraged to attend the ʿīd prayer. There is no other prayer for which women are urged to attend except ʿīd prayer; for example, we do not tell them to go to the masjid for Ẓuhr, ʿAṣr, or Jumuʿah—no, only the ʿīd prayer.

The Prophet ﷺ commanded that al-ʿawātiq (young adult women) and dhawāt al-khudūr (unmarried virgins who normally remain at home) be brought out (to the prayer), but stay away from the prayer area if they were menstruating. 

However, it is obligatory for a woman, if she comes to the masjid or the ʿīd prayer ground, to attend without adornment, without perfume, without raising her voice, without laughing, without swaying in her walk, and without anything that could lead to fitnah. If she does any of these things, she is sinful and will not be rewarded."

text.text: Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

If she does any of these things, she is sinful and will not be rewarded."

text.text: #Scholarly_Anecdotes

text:
Sheikh Ḥammād Al-Anṣārī (may Allāh have mercy on him) said:

“When I arrived at a place near Makkah, I met some Bedouins and asked their leader: ‘What are the pillars of Islam?’

He replied, ‘Ash-Shāfi‘ī, Abū Ḥanīfah, Mālik — and I forgot the fourth one!’

So I said to those around him, ‘Remind him — mention the fourth.’

They said, ‘We have forgotten.’

I then said to them, ‘Aḥmad (ibn Ḥanbal).’

They all exclaimed together, ‘Yes, that’s the one!’”

He added:

“This happened when we went out for da‘wah in the year 1375 AH.”

[Collected in his biography]

messages.from: عَلَىٰ نَهجِ السَّلَفِ || Upon the path of the Salaf 📚
text: Imām Muqbil رحمه الله was asked:

text.text: Is a Muslim obligated to follow a specific madhhab? Please clarify with evidence.

text:
He answered:

"A Muslim is not obligated to follow a specific madhhab. Rather, adhering to a specific madhhab is considered an innovation. Allāh, Glorified and Exalted, says in His Noble Book:

text.text: “Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, and do not follow allies besides Him. Little do you remember.” [Al-A‘rāf: 3]

text: And He, Glorified and Exalted, says:

text.text: “If you dispute over anything, refer it to Allāh and the Messenger, if you truly believe in Allāh and the Last Day.” [An-Nisā’: 59]

text: And He, Glorified and Exalted, says:

text.text: “And in anything over which you differ—the decision thereof is with Allāh.” [ash-Shūrā: 10]

text:
I praise Allāh that the youth today no longer turn except to the Book and the Sunnah. And we praise Allāh that the environment has become easier, for there was a time when no one could say that he was not committed to a madhhab. These madhhabs have stirred up trials and enmity among the Muslims. If you read in al-Bidāyah wan-Nihāyah, you will find disputes between the Ḥanbalīs and the Shāfi‘īs, between the Ḥanbalīs and the Ḥanafīs, and between the Shāfi‘īs and the Ḥanafīs, and other such conflicts—along with fighting and killing that occurred due to adhering to madhhabs. 

Allāh, Glorified and Exalted, says:

text.text: “Whatever the Messenger gives you, take it; and whatever he forbids you from, refrain from it.” [Al-Hashr: 7]
He also said:

text: "As for blind following (taqlīd), my brothers, it has brought great affliction upon the people. How is that? The people abandoned (the way of) the Companions, then they began taking the statements of the four Imāms. After that, they took the statements of the followers of the four Imāms, and then after that, they took the statements of the followers of the followers of the followers."

text.text: And he said:

text: "Aḥmad bin Ḥanbal رحمه الله تعالى, al-Imām ash-Shāfiʿī and al-Imām Mālik and other imāms are not pillars from the pillars of the religion. Rather, they are imāms who are followed (i.e., taken as examples). Especially Imām ash-Shāfiʿī, Imām Aḥmad, and Imām Mālik—may Allāh reward them with good on behalf of Islām. We do not do without their books, but we do not practice taqlīd—we do not blindly follow."

text.text: abridged.
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

He also said:

"As for blind following (taqlīd), my brothers, it has brought great affliction upon the people. How is that? The people abandoned (the way of) the Companions, then they began taking the statements of the four Imāms. After that, they took the statements of the followers of the four Imāms, and then after that, they took the statements of the followers of the followers of the followers."
And he said:

"Aḥmad bin Ḥanbal رحمه الله تعالى, al-Imām ash-Shāfiʿī and al-Imām Mālik and other imāms are not pillars from the pillars of the religion. Rather, they are imāms who are followed (i.e., taken as examples). Especially Imām ash-Shāfiʿī, Imām Aḥmad, and Imām Mālik—may Allāh reward them with good on behalf of Islām. We do not do without their books, but we do not practice taqlīd—we do not blindly follow."
abridged.
Tr: Umm ʿAbdirraḥmān as-Sūdāniyyah

text.text: On Madhhab-following (Taqlīd of a school of thought):

text:
Know, O woman who calls to Allah and teaches (may Allah grant you success), that adhering to a madhhab (school of thought) has no basis in the Sharīʿah. The matter is about following the Qur’an and the Sunnah. These madhāhib came later. Imām al-Bukhārī did not belong to any of the madhāhib, nor did Imām Muslim, nor others from the earlier generations. There was no affiliation to a person. They did not ascribe themselves to Abū Bakr al-Ṣiddīq, nor to ʿUmar, nor to ʿUthmān, nor to ʿAlī. Thus, madhhab-following is something Allah has not sent down any authority for.

And praise be to Allah who has made us see the truth and made madhhabism and partisanship hateful to us.

Indeed, among the blind followers of madhhabs are those who even reject clear evidence. It is said to them: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said such and such. They reply: But my madhhab is Mālikī, or Shāfiʿī… Did Allah command us to follow the Mālikī madhhab, or the Shāfiʿī, or the Ḥanafī, or the Ḥanbalī? And they reject evidence simply because their madhhab is different.

But Allah the Exalted says:

text.text: “Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, and do not follow other guardians besides Him. Little do you remember.”

text:
(Sūrat al-Aʿrāf 7:3)

And He says:

text.text: "So let those beware who oppose his command, lest a trial befall them or a painful punishment befall them.”

text:
(Sūrat al-Nūr 24:63)

And praise be to Allah who has saved us from the trial of partisanships.

And my father (may Allah have mercy on him) said in response to a question (Fatāwā al-Ḥaramayn, no. 490):

text.text: “These madhhabs are a bidʿah with which Allah tested Islām. And I know that many of the doctors, and many of the wearers of cloaks, and many of those with turbans do not like my statement. But if I please my Lord, I do not care about their anger.”

text: Therefore, it is upon the woman who calls to Allah and teaches to be keen on attaching the students and those she calls to the evidences, and never to shift away from them to the statement of so-and-so or the madhhab of so-and-so—while maintaining respect and reverence for the scholars.

text.text: – Shaykha Umm ʿAbdillāh al-Wādiʿiyyah (may Allah preserve her)

text.text: Tawakkul — What It Truly Is and How It Is Achieved

text: A question posed to ash-Shaykhah Umm ʿAbdillāh bint ash-Shaykh Muqbil al-Wādi’yyah حفظها الله

text.text: The Question:

text:
One of my sisters for the sake of Allāh asks:
Our Lord, Glorious and Exalted, has indeed taught us that whosoever puts his trust in Allāh, then He will suffice him. So how can a person place true reliance upon their Lord in a way that pleases Him, and what are the means that help achieve this noble act of worship?

text.text: The Answer:

text:
Understanding the meaning of tawakkul clarifies what is meant by it. It is truly depending upon Allāh, not turning to oneself nor to anyone else besides Him, Glorified and Exalted.

Ibn Rajab, may Allāh have mercy on him, said in Jāmiʿ al-ʿUlūm wa al-Ḥikam (2/497):
“It is the heart's true reliance upon Allāh—Glorified and Exalted—in seeking benefits and warding off harm in all matters of this world and the Hereafter. It is entrusting all affairs to Him and affirming with true faith that no one gives, withholds, harms, or benefits except Him”

Ibn al-Qayyim said in Madārij al-Sālikīn (1/103):

If you ask: What is the meaning of tawakkul and (ʾistiʿānah) seeking assistance?

I say: it is a state of the heart that arises from one’s knowledge of Allāh and belief in His singularity in creation, disposal of affairs, harm, benefit, giving, and withholding. Whatever He wills occurs even if people do not will it, and whatever He does not will does not occur even if people will it. This necessitates reliance upon Him, entrusting matters to Him, finding tranquility in Him, trusting Him, and being certain of His sufficiency for what one relies upon. The heart becomes full of Him, and this does not happen except by His will, whether people will it or not. This resembles the state of a child with its parents regarding what he hopes for and fears, as they are fully capable of fulfilling those for him. Observe how his heart is free from turning to anyone other than his parents and how his concerns are confined to attaining what he intends through them. This is the state of the one who relies upon Allāh.”

Ash-Shaykh Ibn Bāz said in Majmūʿ al-Fatāwā (4/427):

“Tawakkul combines two things:

Firstly: Dependence upon Allāh and belief that He is the Causer of all causes, that His decree is executed, and that He has decreed, recorded, and written all matters, Glorified and Exalted is He.

Secondly: Acting upon the means; for tawakkul does not mean abandoning the means. Rather, it combines acting upon the means while depending on Allāh.” End quote (intended meaning)

text.text: From the things that aid dependence on Allāh alone include:

text:
📍 Beneficial knowledge: For it is a radiant light that guides to the straight path.

📍 ʿAqīdah: Whoever has ʿaqīdah (creed) and knowledge of Allāh, His Names and Attributes, His Messengers and His Books, will attain tawakkul which will increase him in strength.

📍 Faith in Divine Decree (Al-Qadr): My father, ash-Shaykh Muqbil, may Allāh The Exalted have mercy on him, mentioned in Al-Jāmiʿ aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥ fī al-Qadr (14) that among the fruits of belief in Divine Decree are tawakkul, certainty, dependence on Allāh, and submission to Him:
“Say: Nothing shall ever happen to us except what Allāh has ordained for us."  [At-Tawbah : 51]

📍 Knowing the biographies of the Prophets: For they are examples for those who rely upon Allāh.

📍 Frequent remembrance of Allāh, supplication, and seeking forgiveness: Our Lord says: “Those who believed (in the Oneness of Allāh- Islāmic Monotheism), and whose hearts find rest in the remembrance of Allāh: verily, in the remembrance of Allāh do hearts find rest” [Ar-Raʿd : 28]

📍 Humility: Ibn al-Ḥājj said in Al-Madkhal (3/71):
“When humility settles in the heart, all goodness settles within it: compassion, gentleness, mercy, submissiveness, contentment, tawakkul, good assumptions, intense modesty, good manners, renouncing greed, striving against the self, extending goodness, serenity of the heart, preoccupation away from oneself, hastening to do good, and slowing from evil.”
